# MoE Expert-Replacement Design Menu (literature-grounded, adversarially critiqued)

# Expert-Replacement Design Menu: Replacing MoE Experts with Deterministic Code / the Kernel

**Scope.** Five design families were generated from the what-experts-do / pruning-merging / interpretability / cheap-approximation / glm-kernel-grounding literature and each was adversarially critiqued. This document is the synthesis: the factual foundation, a ranked comparison, the 2–3 candidates worth the rigorous design→freeze pipeline, and an honest ledger of what is speculative and what is fundamentally capped.

**One-sentence bottom line.** The literature licenses *pruning* an MoE down ~50% training-free at modest cost, and licenses an *external* deterministic checker over the model's outputs — but it does **not** license replacing a trained expert with hand-written deterministic code at preserved quality; every efficiency win in this space is already delivered kernel-free by REAP-class pruning, so the kernel's only defensible load-bearing role here is as an **external correctness checker**, not as an expert substitute.

---

## 1. What the literature actually establishes about MoE experts

### [established] — cross-verified, multi-source

- **An expert is a token-conditioned FFN shard, not a domain/topic module.** Mixtral's own routing analysis found *no* topic specialization on The Pile; routing tracks token identity / syntax / position with strong consecutive-token locality (14%→28% same-expert repetition, layers 0→15). (2401.04088)
- **FFNs (hence experts) are key-value memories:** keys match input patterns (shallow→semantic with depth), values push output-vocabulary distributions. This is the *only* mechanistic license for any "expert ≈ addressable content store" claim. (Geva 2012.14913)
- **What specialization exists is token-class / operational, not concept-class:** punctuation, proper-noun, conjunction, number/counting, bracket-closing, script/vocabulary experts. (ST-MoE 2202.08906; OLMoE 2409.02060; The Expert Strikes Back 2604.02178)
- **Routing saturates early and is largely a frozen partition:** ~60% of top-8 decisions fixed after ~1% of pretraining. Deterministic hash routing is competitive with learned routing (Hash Layers) — bounding how much semantics the router can be credited with. (OLMoE; Roller 2021)
- **~50% of expert mass is removable training-free at modest cost — IF you score by conditional impact, not frequency.** REAP (gate-value × activation-norm, conditional on activation): GLM-4.5-Air 92.6% coding retention at 50% removal (weakest of its set; Qwen3-Coder 97.6%). Frequency-pruning *collapses* the same model to 0.011. (REAP 2510.13999, full-text)
- **Merging experts is a proven failure at LLM/generative scale.** Replacing input-dependent router mixing r(x) with a constant convex combination has irreducible error ∝ Var[r(x)]·expert-gap ("functional subspace collapse"); HC-SMoE hits 0.008 WildBench at 50%. This is *fundamental*, not capability-limited. (REAP Thm 1)
- **Explanation faithfulness is poor:** rigorously-evaluated neuron/expert labels hit F1≈0.6 with "little to no causal efficacy"; a legible-sounding label is **not** a compilable spec. (2309.10312)
- **Super-experts exist and are catastrophic to touch:** pruning 3 of 6,144 (down_proj massive-activation carriers) degenerates Qwen3-30B. Any replacement design needs a model-specific exclusion pass. (2507.23279)
- **Cold ≠ useless:** rare experts encode indispensable long-tail information; aggregate PPL hides their loss. (2604.23036)
- **Shared experts carry common knowledge:** disabling GLM-5.2's always-on shared expert costs +33.5% loss (DeepSeekMoE) — highest-stakes, lowest-redundancy, worst thing to approximate. (2401.06066)
- **Deterministic solver offload works at the text seam:** Logic-LM +39.2% over prompting, +18.4% over CoT. This is the strongest "engine owns correctness" result — and it is *also the ceiling any in-network kernel checker must beat* (kernel-as-text null). (2305.12295)

### [established-but-bounded] — true, with a caveat that flips its use

- **Deterministic shallow rules absorb 68–79% of transformer top-1 next-token behavior** (N-gram, 2407.12034) — but the 21–32% residual *is the hard part*, and this is a whole-model next-token statistic, **not** a single-expert-contribution bound.
- **Trained lookup/memory layers match MoE at matched compute+params, biggest gains on factual tasks** (Memory Layers 2412.09764; PEER; UltraMemV2) — but every success is **trained from scratch at matched params**, never a training-free retrofit of a frozen expert.
- **Fine-grained (256-expert, GLM-5.2-regime) MoEs show *more* specialization than coarse Mixtral** (DeepSeekMoE, OLMoE, EASY-EP 50%-retain @ 2.99× on DeepSeek-family) — but "more" still means input-cluster-conditioned function pieces, not concept modules, and "Myth of Expert Specialization" argues routing reflects embedding geometry, not domain expertise.

### [speculative] — no prior art; the programme's actual open bets

- **Replacing a trained production-MoE expert with hand-written deterministic code at preserved perplexity** — explicit null across the interpretability and cheap-approximation facet searches. No existence proof.
- **A kernel concept-gate selecting the legible/replaceable subset better than a kernel-free activation gate.**
- **A kernel-tabulated / kernel-seeded value store beating both pruning and kernel-as-text at matched budget.**

### Fundamental (not capability-limited) ceilings

1. **Merge-to-constant collapse** (REAP Thm 1) — any rung that replaces r(x) with a constant/averaged value inherits Var[r(x)]·gap error. GLM-5.2's from-scratch near-orthogonal experts (pairwise cos ≈ 0) *maximize* this gap.
2. **Continuous-manifold vs finite-table mismatch** — an expert is a nonlinear map over a continuous hidden-state manifold; exact tabulation exists only on a genuinely closed, narrow gated input set. Output-rarity ≠ input-discretizability.
3. **Explanation unfaithfulness residue** — superposition persists at fixed width; labels are not specs. Fundamental at fixed expert width.
4. **Late-layer output-token routing** — late layers route on the *predicted* token, so any input-concept-keyed lookup/gate structurally mispredicts there.

## 2. Ranked comparison of expert-replacement families

Ranked by *honest defensible value* (grounded yield − dominated-by-baseline − kernel-necessity). Every family's critique returned **marginal**; the ranking is by how much survives when the kernel claim and the throughput claim are priced honestly. Retention/throughput figures are cross-model transfers (STIPULATED) unless marked MEASURED.

| # | Family | Mechanism | Honest expected yield (retention × cut) | Correctness risk | Kernel connection | Cheapest test | Verdict | Prior-art status |
|---|--------|-----------|------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|----------------|---------|------------------|
| 1 | **Utilization-tiered retirement** (REAP-on-GLM-5.2 + fallback ladder) | REAP conditional-impact saliency → retire ~50% routed experts, R0 drop+renorm; R1–R4 fallback rungs | **~92–97% retention × ~47% disk-GB cut** (grounded on GLM-4.5-Air; int4-stacked unmeasured). Throughput on the disk-bound box is a **hit-rate delta ~1.1–1.4×**, NOT the EASY-EP 2.99× (that's a GPU-fit effect) | Medium at R0 (pruning is well-characterized); high at R1–R3 (they re-incur merge-collapse) | Weak/decorative. Efficiency needs **no** kernel. Only honest role: R4 kernel-as-external-checker | ~$2–3 saliency-flatness gate + hit-rate-vs-union measurement + int4-stacked retention probe (reuses colibri STATS/LOOKA/TOPK) | **Promising** — but only as "honestly-priced REAP + optional checker," kernel non-load-bearing | Re-application of REAP/EASY-EP/STUN; kernel rungs novel but refuted-in-limit |
| 2 | **Function-identification (FISR)** as a *measurement/falsifier* | Weight-space output-entropy screen → activation-fidelity → causal-DLA gate; identify the legible minority | As an **experiment**: a clean yes/no + size on "are GLM-5.2 decoder experts compilable?" As a **system**: ~1.0–1.06× efficiency, below REAP and below just enabling MTP | Low as a measurement; high as substitution (F1≈0.6 faithfulness cap; decoder penalty) | Partial. Bulk of legible experts (copy/positional/punctuation) are token-syntax objects the NSM kernel has nothing to say about | STAGE 0 near-$0 weight-space entropy pass (late-layer-only valid); STAGE 1 ~$30–80 fidelity+causal-DLA on dump patch | **Promising as a falsifier, dead-end as an efficiency system** | Novel composition; every rung individually dominated (REAP / GBNF / transcoders) |
| 3 | **Kernel-grounding (KCES)** — reframed to kernel-as-selector + output checker | Concept-coverage as a *saliency prior* for a REAP-style deletion pruner; R0 grounding-veto at the output logit seam | Deletion arm = REAP numbers; kernel's marginal delta over activation-norm saliency is the single testable claim, **modal outcome = tie** | Low if deletion-only + output-seam checker; the in-network gate is mechanism-blocked (no text at a mid-stack hidden state) | The runnable per-layer gate is **kernel-free hidden-space cosine** by construction; kernel-space gate needs a trained bridge (forfeits training-free + triggers Law-1) | Tier-0 effective-rank histogram on proxy MoE (DeepSeek-16B/Qwen3-30B); one clean claim: concept-weighted selection > REAP saliency at matched sparsity, validated by full-generation KL | **Marginal** — one clean novel claim survives (kernel-as-selector); value-seam kernel is inert | Gate = REAP re-badged; R0 = Logic-LM relocated back to its own null |
| 4 | **Cluster-Distill-to-Lookup (CDL)** | Distill saliency-selected expert clusters into input-conditioned lookup (hashed-KV → low-rank → product-key → kernel-seeded) | Headline (≥95% × 50% cut, ~5×) **internally inconsistent**: small surrogate → no deletion → no disk win; matched-param surrogate → not RAM-resident on 64 GB box. Realistic = ties REAP, kernel-free | R2 analytically dead (near-orthogonal experts); splice point is post-sum additive → cannot delete bytes as written; shared-expert target is the *worst* lookup case | Only at R4; a memory layer IS a key-value store so kernel vectors are natural values — but modal result is kernel-vs-generic tie | Stage-0 free analytic Var[r(x)]×gap + cosine kill of R2; Stage-1 one-layer product-key vs REAP at matched budget | **Marginal→dead-end** — dominated by "just run dense-KD (2605.28207) or REAP" | Recombination of Memory-Layers/MC-SMoE/transcoders; novel cell is the unproven one |
| 5 | **Deterministic Routing Short-Circuit (DRS)** | Predict routing from surface features → prefetch (DRS-0/1, byte-identical) / memoize MoE output by token-class (DRS-2, byte-removing) | DRS-0/1 throughput **≈0 on the measured saturated disk** (prefetch overlaps, doesn't remove bytes). DRS-2 realistic ~1.04×, *below* REAP's 1.67× and below its own stipulate | DRS-0/1 zero (router authoritative, SHA-256 guard). DRS-2 = per-class merge-to-constant → REAP Thm-1 collapse; trapped between "safe→tiny" and "material→corrupts" | Explicitly **kernel-free** — it IS the a1/a2 deflator the kernel routing claim must beat | **$0** offline re-analysis of committed P0 routing traces (FALSIFIER-1: marginal predictable-AND-not-resident fraction above the 0.76 static hot set) | **Marginal as a lever; valuable as a $0 measurement + deflator** | DRS-0/1 = Pre-gated MoE/PILOT/Hash-Layers re-application; DRS-2 = refuted-in-limit merge |

**Cross-cutting verdict:** no family delivers a kernel-load-bearing efficiency win. The efficiency thesis is carried entirely by pruning (REAP), which is kernel-free. The kernel's honest home is the correctness thesis at the **output seam** (Logic-LM / Law 3), not inside the MoE sum.

---

## 3. The 2–3 candidates to route to the Fable + GPT-5.6 design→freeze pipeline

Selected for: (a) grounded or near-$0 falsifiable, (b) fails safe, (c) advances a real programme track, (d) an honest modal outcome that is still *reportable* if the kernel earns nothing.

### Candidate A — Honestly-priced REAP-on-GLM-5.2 (compression track + GLM-5.2 north-star)
**What it is.** Utilization-tiered retirement collapsed to its defensible core: REAP conditional-impact saliency → R0 drop+renorm at 50% routed experts; **kernel rides only as an optional R4 external checker** over the pruned model's concept-bearing outputs. R1/R2/R3 demoted to explicitly-labelled *losing control arms* that test REAP's Thm-1 prediction.

**First experiment (staged, ~$2–3 → ~$30).**
1. **Saliency-flatness gate** (~$2–3, reuses colibri STATS/LOOKA/TOPK, generation-side, DRAFT=0): compute REAP saliency per expert on a few MoE layers over ~256 broad calibration sequences. Falsifier: if the bottom-25% saliency tail is indistinguishable from the median, the family is dead before any prune.
2. **Hit-rate-vs-union measurement**: price the *actual* throughput as a cache hit-rate delta — NOT the EASY-EP GPU-fit number.
3. **int4-stacked retention probe** on a small **broad** suite (never PPL alone; honesty-first scoring, wrong = −2..−5): measure retention on the already-int4 model, do not extrapolate REAP's non-int4 datum. Include an MTP-on acceptance check at a candidate retention.

**Why it fits.** Directly serves the **dimension-collapse compression track** (training-free ~47% disk-GB cut) and the **GLM-5.2 north-star** (shrink GLM-5.2). Honest ceiling: "REAP retires ~50% of routed experts for a ~47% disk cut at retention-to-be-measured int4-stacked, modest hit-rate throughput bump, kernel as optional external checker — the kernel replaces no expert." **Hard rule for freeze: broad calibration set, NOT a kernel-concept-narrow one** (a 24-concept corpus is the c4-calibrated-a-coding-model failure in reverse).

### Candidate B — GLM-5.2 Expert-Legibility Measurement (F1-K carrier mechanism + honest-science)
**What it is.** FISR-IDENTIFY and KCES Tier-0 merged into a single first-class deliverable, not a mere gate: **"Does GLM-5.2's decoder MoE contain a legible × causally-confirmed × high-fidelity routed-expert subset, and how large is it?"** A clean falsifier whose null is itself a reportable, publishable datum.

**First experiment (~$0–80, reuses the F1-K dump patch at glm.c:1270).**
- **Stage 0** (near-$0): on a proxy 256-expert MoE (DeepSeek-MoE-16B / Qwen3-30B-A3B — avoids the 370 GB estate and the fact GLM-5.2 F1-K dumps don't exist yet), histogram the **effective-rank of each expert's output-vector *set*** over its calibration-cluster inputs (drop the ill-defined "output-distribution entropy" — an FFN emits a vector, not a distribution). Restrict any logit-lens screen to **late layers only** (the unembedding projection is invalid at layers 3–40).
- **Stage 1** (~$30–80 if a low-rank tail exists): fit the surrogate menu on the low-rank candidates; measure held-out **causal-DLA null-on-controls** against the F1≈0.6 illegibility floor.

**Why it fits.** Reuses the **F1-K carrier mechanism** (dump patch, moe() splice) at near-zero marginal cost; answers the load-bearing precondition for *every* substitution design in this menu; needs no Law-1 amendment. On current evidence (decoder penalty + the probe's 93.92% universal-mass concentration + F1≈0.6) the honest prior is that the answer **leans NO** — which is exactly why measuring it cheaply, once, is high-value: it either unlocks or forecloses the entire in-network substitution direction.

### Candidate C (lower priority) — Kernel-as-Selector: concept-coverage saliency vs REAP saliency (kernel track, single clean claim)
**What it is.** The one place the kernel has structure REAP's frequency×norm lacks: use KoT concept-coverage as a **saliency prior for a deletion pruner** (not a value in the residual stream). One falsifiable claim: *"concept-coverage-weighted selection retains more quality than REAP activation-norm saliency at matched sparsity."*

**First experiment.** Deletion-only (fails safe to REAP's known behavior, dodges merge/frequency nulls by construction), on the proxy MoE, validated by **full-generation KL under distribution shift** — not single-expert prefill KL (prefill over-predicts replaceability). Must beat REAP+INT4 at matched disk+FLOPs.

**Why it fits.** The **kernel track** with the honest modal prediction stated up front (a tie with REAP saliency), so a null is a real datum. It keeps the kernel *out* of activations entirely — no Law-1 amendment, no F1-K Gate-0 dependency. Route only if Candidate A's flatness gate confirms a droppable tail exists.

**Sequencing.** Run Candidate A step-1 (flatness) and Candidate B Stage-0 (proxy legibility) and the DRS **$0 routing-trace re-analysis** in parallel — all are near-free and each independently gates a whole design direction. Candidate C is contingent on A step-1 passing.

---

## 4. Honest caveats

### What is speculative (no prior art, tag before any premise use)
- **Every "replace an expert with deterministic code" rung** (FISR R-substitution, KCES R1/R3, CDL R4, DRS-2). The facet searches returned an explicit null; there is no existence proof that a trained-expert substitute preserves quality at matched budget.
- **The kernel beating a kernel-free baseline anywhere on this seam.** The programme's own ledger is 0-for-5 on runtime-judgment interfaces and 4× content-not-structure deflations. The modal outcome at the *value* seam is a tie with a generic lookup / activation-cluster gate.
- **Kernel-seeded compositional generalization** (CDL R4's entailment-closure claim) — the only argument for a kernel value store over a frozen table, entirely unmeasured.

### GLM-5.2-specific unknowns that block the yield estimates
- **Every retention/throughput number is a cross-model transfer**, none measured on GLM-5.2. The GLM-4.5-Air 92.6% datum is same-lineage but same-family ≠ same-model, and it is REAP's *weakest* result.
- **int4 stacking is unpriced.** All these designs run on the already-int4 colibri estate; the retention data are non-int4, and 50%-prune-∘-int4 joint error is uncharacterized (HOBBIT only claims <1% at <20% quantized, never 50%-pruned-AND-int4).
- **MTP interaction is masked by the DRAFT=0 probe.** The int8 MTP head (layer 78, 39–59% acceptance → 2.2–2.8 tok/forward) was trained against the full expert set; pruning layers 4–77 shifts the hidden states it verifies, and DRAFT=0 deliberately hides this — so no probe here prices real deployment throughput.
- **Disk-bound throughput physics.** On the i4i.2xlarge (disk = 80% wall-time), retiring experts at fixed k=8 does **not** reduce per-token bytes streamed — only union size / cache hit-rate. The EASY-EP 2.99× is a GPU-memory-fit effect that does not transfer. Realistic bump: ~1.1–1.4×. The 5× Amdahl ceiling requires survivors to fit in RAM (~12% retention), far past any quality-safe fraction.
- **Super-expert exclusion list is empty for GLM-5.2.** Super-experts are model-specific down_proj outliers; none identified yet, so every substitution/prune design currently ships with a live degeneration hazard until an identification pass runs.
- **F1-K has not produced results.** The "already-collected MoE-input dumps" several designs assume do not exist yet (still in inertness-gate bring-up as of 2026-07-17); and those dumps are moe() *inputs*, so per-expert output analysis needs an expert-forward recompute (the int4 weights or a proxy), not free CPU-minutes.

### Where the ceiling is fundamental, not capability-limited
- **Merge / average / constant-substitute collapse** (REAP Thm 1): irreducible error ∝ Var[r(x)]·expert-gap. GLM-5.2's near-orthogonal from-scratch experts maximize the gap. R1/R2/R3-style rungs are *dominated by clean drop+renorm* — the fallback ladder's "cheapest→most-faithful" ordering is backwards.
- **Continuous manifold vs finite table**: a cold expert still sees continuous hidden states, not a discrete concept id. "Narrow support ⇒ finite table is exact" conflates output-rarity with input-discretizability. Exact only on a genuinely closed gated input set — which routing does not provide.
- **Late-layer output-token routing**: input-concept-keyed gates/lookups mispredict structurally in late layers. Confines any substitution to early/mid MoE layers — which collides with the ST-MoE "operational experts live in first *and last* layers" prior, shrinking the addressable set.
- **Explanation-faithfulness residue** (F1≈0.6): superposition at fixed expert width. A legible label is never a compilable spec; validation must be held-out output KL, and for the in-network case, *full-generation* KL under shift.
- **The kernel-as-text null (Law 2) + REAP+INT4 baseline**: every in-network rung must beat prepending the same kernel content as text *and* the near-lossless free pruning baseline at matched disk+FLOPs. This double bar is where every kernel-in-the-weights claim currently dies.

### The honest strategic read
The expert seam is the **wrong seam** for the kernel. The correctness thesis (kernel/rules-engine as a verified oracle) belongs at the **output/logits boundary** — GBNF for structure, logit-bias/draft-injection of an engine-computed token for value (the Logic-LM +39% / D7 rung) — where it needs no weight surgery, incurs no super-expert or faithfulness-ceiling risk, and has the only published positive number. The efficiency thesis belongs to **pruning**, which is kernel-free. The genuinely worthwhile near-term deliverables are therefore **measurements**: the ~$2–3 saliency-flatness gate, the ~$0–80 proxy legibility histogram, and the **$0** routing-trace re-analysis — each of which forecloses or unlocks an entire design direction, and each of which is a reportable datum whether the kernel earns anything or not.

# Coordinator steering note — dual-model cross-read (2026-07-17)

Two independent models (Fable + GPT-5.6) gave a SUBJECTIVE steering read of the synthesis above before it is
banked as programme direction. Labelled OPINION; does not override mechanical verdicts. They converge strongly;
the coordinator synthesis:

**BANK the central conclusion, but not categorically.** Both agree the broad "compile an expert into hand-written
code" bet is dead (polysemantic continuous functions; hidden-state/text bridge; merge-collapse; the
anti-correlation below). BUT "no existence proof" is not "refuted": the *fundamental* ceilings (REAP Thm 1,
manifold) kill only CONSTANT / merge substitution, not gated conditional substitution. State it that precisely.

**Promote the anti-correlation argument to the headline (Fable).** *Safe-to-substitute = cheap-to-delete =
already-hot* — the substitution delta over pruning vanishes exactly where substitution is possible. This is the
near-fatal core, currently buried in the KCES critique.

**Priority: Candidate A first (both).** The ~$2-3 saliency-flatness gate + the **$0 routing-trace re-analysis**
lead; they interrogate the real GLM-5.2/int4 north-star and A's falsifier gates C for free. Sequence ruthlessly —
a flatness histogram must NOT license the 50% headline; measure hit-rate-vs-union, a broad-suite retention probe on
the int4-STACKED model, and an MTP-on acceptance check.

**Candidate B: run cheap, bank WEAK (both).** The "leans NO" prior is a reason to foreclose the direction for
~$0-80, not to skip it — but Stage-0 runs on a PROXY MoE, and the doc itself refuses cross-model retention
transfer, so a proxy legibility histogram licenses far less about GLM-5.2 than "unlocks/forecloses" claims
(GPT-5.6: run B only when it can piggyback on real GLM expert-forward data).

**PROTECT F1-K's registered readout (both, emphatic).** Do NOT let "wrong seam for *replacement*" bleed into
killing F1-K: the carrier mechanism tests whether kernel vectors can ride in activations AT ALL — a different
question from expert substitution — the instrument is mostly built (B reuses its dump patch), and abandoning a
pre-registered experiment mid-bring-up because an *adjacent* bet is marginal would be prior-laundering. Run F1-K to
its registered readout.

**Apply the doc's own skepticism to the seam it steers TOWARD (Fable — the key underweight).** The redirect to the
output/logit seam rests on Logic-LM's +39%, a text-seam, formalizable-benchmark number whose transfer to
open-ended generation-checking on a pruned int4 GLM-5.2 is *exactly as unmeasured* as the expert-seam numbers the
doc deflates. Before banking "the kernel's home is the output seam," the checker claim needs its OWN priced
falsifier (vs kernel-as-text, on the real deployment config) — migrate the test, not the optimism.

**Stronger-C (GPT-5.6): kernel as an OFFLINE correctness constraint on static pruning** — does kernel-derived
closure + adversarial cases preserve kernel-covered capabilities at a MORE aggressive compression point than REAP
alone? Deletion-only, compared against broad-calibration REAP + equally-sized generic capability constraints. This
is the one expert-seam role that survives; keep C as a small post-A attribution ablation.

**DRS needs a real look (GPT-5.6 catch).** [Coordinator note: GPT-5.6 flagged the DRS critique as mispaired — this
was a coordinator doc-assembly bug, now FIXED below; the underlying DRS critique is DRS-specific and marginal.]
Substantively both still rate DRS marginal (DRS-0/1 byte-identical but ~0 throughput on the measured saturated
disk; DRS-2 is merge-collapse), BUT routing/cache policy is the most plausible overlooked KERNEL-FREE systems
opportunity — worth the $0 routing-trace re-analysis under Candidate A regardless.

**DROP as programme bets:** hand-written expert substitution, shared-expert modification, in-network kernel
value-vector injection, product-key retrofit, the R1-R3 fallback rungs, DRS-2, CDL. **KEEP:** honestly-priced
REAP/int4/MTP dimension-collapse (kernel-free); the three cheap measurements; F1-K to its registered readout; the
D7/logit-bias output-seam rung — but priced against its own falsifier before it is called the kernel's "home".

# Appendix — the five design families (design + its CORRECT adversarial critique)

## Utilization-Tiered Expert Retirement
**One-liner:** Retire the lowest-marginal-utility GLM-5.2 routed experts (REAP-saliency, never frequency) and back each with a cheapest-adequate fallback rung — renorm-drop -> shared-expert absorb -> activation-aware low-rank stub -> kernel-tabulated cold tail -> kernel-as-checker — buying disk-GB and offloaded throughput while the kernel de-risks the accuracy loss.

**Mechanism:** Per MoE layer (75 of GLM-5.2's 78 layers; first 3 are dense, skip), score each of the 256 routed experts on a calibration set with REAP saliency = gate-value x expert-activation-norm computed CONDITIONALLY ON ACTIVATION (decouples impact from how often it fires) [cheap-approximation/pruning-merging: REAP 2510.13999, verified full-text]. Retire the bottom-f-fraction tail; renormalize the surviving top-k gate mass so the router still sums to 1 (R0, REAP's own recipe). GLM-5.2 specifics: (i) colibri is disk-bound at ~11.4 GB/token (75 x 8 x ~19 MB), and throughput is inverse-linear in expert bytes read [MEASURED: northstar §1.2], so retiring f of the experts removes them from the streamed-from-disk union -> directly fewer loads -> more tok/s — the win is memory/bandwidth, NOT dense FLOPs (top-k stays 8 over survivors). (ii) Run the probe at DRAFT=0 (MTP off) so retired experts do not leak into the draft-verify expert union and confound the read. (iii) BEFORE retiring, build a data-agnostic super-expert exclusion list (down_proj massive-activation-outlier carriers; pruning 3/6144 degenerated Qwen3-30B) [pruning-merging: Super Experts 2507.23279] and exclude the shared expert(s) (they carry common knowledge; disabling raises loss +33.5%) [what-experts-do/glm-grounding: DeepSeekMoE 2401.06066]. Fallback ladder for retired slots, cheapest->most-faithful: R1 route retired mass onto the always-on shared expert (GLM-5.2 has it; speculative — may already be saturated); R2 activation-aware low-rank stub (ASVD/SVD-LLM/CALR — plain SVD is a known negative) for near-threshold experts; R3 for the LOWEST-entropy cold experts replace the output with a deterministic kernel-derived value vector keyed on concept id (Geva key-value + Memory-Layers licence) — feasible precisely because coldness correlates with narrow empirical input support, the one regime where finite tabulation is faithful; R4 run the kernel-engine as a grounding-checker over concept-bearing tokens the retirement touched (Logic-LM +39% analogue). Saliency must be computed GENERATION-side, not prefill: prefill routing over-predicts replaceability [cheap-approximation: Myth-of-Specialization 2604.09780].

**Target Experts:** Low-MARGINAL-UTILITY routed experts, identified per-layer by REAP conditional-activation saliency (gate x activation-norm), NOT the cold/rarely-routed set — raw-frequency pruning is the built-in null and is expected to collapse (frequency-pruned Qwen3-Coder-480B -> 0.011 coding, total collapse) [pruning-merging: REAP Table 3]. Scope: the 75 MoE layers x 256 routed experts (~19,200; README also says 21,504 — read exact count from config.json at P0). EXCLUDED from the retire pool: (a) shared expert(s) — always-on common-knowledge carriers [glm-grounding: DeepSeekMoE]; (b) a data-agnostic super-expert exclusion list (down_proj outlier carriers) [Super Experts 2507.23279]; (c) whatever a long-tail probe flags as rare-but-indispensable [pruning-merging: 2604.23036]. Kernel-native variant additionally DOWN-weights saliency for experts whose activations the kernel can ground (safest to retire, since the kernel can substitute their grounding role); its R3 rung further selects the lowest-INPUT-ENTROPY members of the cold tail (narrow gated support = only regime where deterministic tabulation is exact) [interpretability: replacement feasible only where empirical input set is small].

**Expected Yield:** Headline STIPULATED (transfer to GLM-5.2 unmeasured): at 50% routed-expert retirement, ~92-97% task retention x ~47% disk-GB cut x ~2-3x offloaded throughput. Reasoning + grounding flags: (1) DISK: routed experts are ~370 GB of the ~380 GB int4 footprint (~97%) [MEASURED: northstar §0/§1.2], so 50% retire -> 370->~195 GB total = ~47% cut. Mechanism GROUNDED (REAP deletes experts); magnitude STIPULATED. (2) RETENTION: GROUNDED datum is REAP on GLM-4.5-Air (same lineage as 5.2) = 0.553/0.597 = 92.6% coding at 50% (weakest domain in REAP's set), 96-98% on math/other [pruning-merging, full-text]; STIPULATED that 5.2 (larger -> plausibly MORE redundant) meets-or-beats this. At the safe 25% variant, ~99% (PuzzleMoE DeepSeek-MoE -0.2%; Not-All-Experts drop-2/8 small) [cheap-approximation/pruning-merging]. (3) THROUGHPUT: GROUNDED analogue EASY-EP on DeepSeek-R1/V3 256-expert = 2.99x at 50% retention [glm-grounding 2504.06792]; STIPULATED transfer; on colibri's disk-bound box the inverse-linear-in-bytes-read law makes this the real win, dense-FLOP win ~0. (4) The frequency-null arm is expected to COLLAPSE (0.011) — that contrast, not the absolute number, is what the design must demonstrate. Kernel rungs R3/R4 add NO efficiency (they are correctness/de-risking) and their yield is UNGROUNDED/speculative.

**Correctness Risk:** Ordered by severity: (1) Frequency != utility — if the criterion degrades to raw coldness the model collapses (REAP Table 3: 0.011); MITIGATE by using conditional-activation saliency, keep frequency as the explicit null arm. (2) Super-expert catastrophe — retiring the wrong 3-of-thousands degenerates the model [2507.23279]; MITIGATE with the data-agnostic exclusion list built first. (3) Calibration-domain conditioning — REAP c4-calibrated a coding model -> incoherent output; retention is PER-DOMAIN, "near-lossless" is never general; a kernel-concept-weighted calibration set risks biasing retention toward concept tokens and hurting general fluency. (4) Silent long-tail loss — rare experts carry indispensable long-tail info [2604.23036]; finite-calibration saliency under-samples the tail and aggregate PPL hides it -> eval MUST include rare-capability probes + honesty-first scoring (wrong = -2..-5), never PPL alone. (5) Prefill over-predicts replaceability — deep-layer prefill routing collapses but pruned experts still hurt generation [2604.09780]; compute saliency generation-side; the programme's STATS/LOOKA prefill fingerprints over-predict. (6) R1 shared-expert absorption may overload an already-load-bearing shared expert (+33.5% loss if disabled) -> speculative, could worsen. (7) Router renormalization + MTP interaction: retired experts inside the draft-verify union shift acceptance; isolate with DRAFT=0 in the probe. (8) R3 kernel-tabulation is exact only on a closed gated input set — an expert is a nonlinear map over a continuous manifold, so faithful only where input support is genuinely narrow; MUST beat kernel-as-text (Law 2).

**Kernel Connection:** Honest: the EFFICIENCY win of this family does NOT require the kernel — REAP/EASY-EP already deliver retire+renorm on this architecture. The kernel attaches three additive, de-risking ways, each of which must beat the kernel-as-text null (Law 2): (a) KERNEL-CONDITIONED SALIENCY + CALIBRATION — kernel concept coverage defines the calibration distribution so retained experts span the programme's concept space, and kernel-groundable low-saliency experts become the safest retire targets (this directly addresses risk-3 calibration-domain sensitivity). (b) R3 KERNEL-TABULATED COLD TAIL — the one genuine 'replace-expert-with-deterministic-code' rung: for the lowest-input-entropy retired experts, emit a deterministic kernel-derived value vector (Geva key-value + Memory-Layers licence); this is the concept-kernel acting as the value store of a lookup slot. (c) R4 KERNEL-AS-CHECKER — the engine catches factual/logical errors the retirement introduces on concept-bearing tokens (Law 3, Logic-LM +39% analogue), joining the efficiency thesis to the correctness thesis in one artifact. Caveat: the family is fundamentally a pruning family that the kernel DECORATES; only R3 is a load-bearing kernel-replacement claim and it has no published precedent, so the kernel's necessity here is weak — its strongest honest role is (c) checker, not (b) replacement.

**Cheapest Test:** MEASUREMENT-ONLY falsifier (~$2-3, no full prune, reuses the eu-west-2 colibri box's existing STATS/LOOKA/TOPK instruments — TOPK is already the 'shrink lever'): compute REAP saliency (gate-value x expert-activation-norm, conditional-on-activation) per expert on ONE or a few GLM-5.2 MoE layers over ~256 calibration sequences, GENERATION-side, DRAFT=0, and inspect the per-layer saliency DISTRIBUTION. FALSIFIER: if saliency is FLAT — the bottom-25%-saliency experts have conditional impact indistinguishable from the median (no droppable tail) — the utilization family is dead for GLM-5.2 before any prune is run. If a low-saliency tail exists, the cheap confirmatory is: hard-drop that bottom 25% (R0), renormalize, run a small held-out suite + the frequency-pruned CONTROL arm (expected to collapse per REAP), and measure the retention gap between marginal-utility and frequency selection. The flatness check is the first and cheapest gate — pure measurement, no eval run.

**Prior Art:** CLOSEST: REAP (2510.13999, Cerebras, full-text verified) — router-weighted activation pruning with the exact GLM-4.5-Air 50%-retention numbers; this design is essentially REAP applied to GLM-5.2 PLUS the fallback ladder and kernel rungs. EASY-EP (2504.06792) — DeepSeek-family 256-expert (GLM-5.2's regime), 50%-retention at 2.99x throughput = the throughput analogue. STUN (2409.06211) — prune-then-unstructured stacking (a further compression rung). Not-All-Experts-Equal (2402.14800) — calibration-set drop + dynamic skip. HOBBIT / MoE-Infinity — cold-expert PRECISION ladder = the direct analogue for a per-expert cheapest-adequate-implementation ladder. Guardrails: Super Experts (2507.23279) exclusion list; Long-Tailed Expert Info (2604.23036) negative. NOVELTY: the prune backbone is a RE-APPLICATION (REAP/EASY-EP), not novel. Genuinely novel = the deterministic/kernel fallback rungs — pruning-merging §4c explicitly reports NO published precedent for replacing a trained MoE expert with hand-written deterministic code; R3 (kernel-tabulated cold tail) is that unprecedented bet, R1 (shared-expert absorption of retired mass) and R4 (kernel-checker over pruned tokens) have no direct published analogue found.

**Epistemic Tag:** PLAUSIBLE for the efficiency core, SPECULATIVE for the kernel-replacement rungs. Established: 50% of expert mass is marginal-utility-removable at modest cost on the DeepSeek/GLM family with a calibration set only (REAP full-text, GLM-4.5-Air datum); frequency-pruning collapses (REAP Table 3); super-experts and long-tail experts must be excluded. PLAUSIBLE (unmeasured on GLM-5.2 itself — needs P0): that GLM-5.2 inherits GLM-4.5-Air's ~92-97% retention and that colibri's disk-bound regime converts the byte cut into ~2-3x tok/s. SPECULATIVE (no prior art): R1 shared-expert absorption, R3 kernel-tabulation of the cold tail (the only true expert->code claim), and that any kernel rung beats the kernel-as-text null. Why not 'established' overall: every quantitative retention/throughput number is a cross-model transfer, not a GLM-5.2 measurement, and the kernel's efficiency contribution is currently zero above plain REAP.

### Adversarial critique — verdict: **MARGINAL**

**Feasibility Glm52:** Instrumentation is feasible on colibri: computing REAP saliency (gate x conditional-activation-norm) needs a moe() dump patch of the same size as the already-scoped F1-K construction patch (glm.c:1270, ~40-60 lines), so the mechanism is buildable and DRAFT=0 correctly keeps retired experts out of the MTP draft-verify union for the PROBE. Shared-expert exclusion is correct. Renorm-drop (R0) is validated for exactly this router family — GLM-4.5-Air is sigmoid-gated + per-expert-bias + loss-free-balance and is IN REAP's tested set — though the exact colibri gate combination (g_norm_topk on/off) must be re-verified at bring-up. BUT the headline throughput claim fails on colibri physics: per-token I/O = L_moe x k x 19MB = 75 x 8 x 19MB (northstar 1.2), and retirement leaves L_moe, k=8, and 19MB all UNCHANGED — the router still selects top-8 among survivors. Retiring experts shrinks the UNION (fewer distinct experts can ever be loaded), which raises cache HIT RATE, but does NOT reduce per-token bytes streamed. So the disk-bound win is only the marginal hit-rate delta, not the retired fraction. To convert retirement into a big tok/s win you must retire enough that survivors fit in RAM (~45GB usable on a 64GB box => retain ~12%, retire ~88%), which is far past any quality-safe fraction. Two further GLM-5.2-specific breaks: (1) MTP — the int8 MTP head at layer 78 was trained against the full expert set; pruning layers 1-77 shifts the hidden states it verifies, plausibly dropping the 39-59% acceptance that gives colibri 2.2-2.8 tok/forward, and the DRAFT=0 probe deliberately hides this, so the probe cannot price real deployment throughput. (2) int4 — colibri already applies int4, so this is 50%-prune STACKED on int4; the retention datum comes from non-int4 REAP and the joint error is unmeasured.

**Yield Realism:** Only the disk-GB cut is realistic. (1) DISK-GB: ~47% at 50% retire is sound arithmetic (routed experts ~97% of the int4 footprint) and is the one honest headline. (2) RETENTION: the 92-97% figure is a straight transfer of REAP's GLM-4.5-Air 0.553/0.597 = 92.6% coding datum, at non-int4 precision, on a BROAD calibration set. Stacking 50%-prune on int4 compounds error beyond additive (HOBBIT only claims <1% loss when <20% of experts go INT4/INT2, not 50%-pruned AND fully int4), and the design's own kernel-conditioned-calibration variant makes it WORSE: a 24-concept kernel corpus is the c4-calibrated-a-coding-model failure (REAP) in reverse — it biases retention toward kinship/senses/molecules and would wreck general fluency. So realistic int4-stacked general retention is plausibly below 92.6%, unknown until measured. (3) THROUGHPUT 2-3x: not realistic on colibri. EASY-EP's 2.99x is a GPU-memory-FIT effect (pruned experts stop being offloaded); colibri stays disk-STREAMING-bound after a 50% prune because 195GB still does not fit in 32-64GB RAM. Within the design's own Amdahl frame (F1 1.3, d=0.8), 2-3x needs r~=0.6-0.75 miss-byte elimination; a 50% union cut against a 5-12% cache moves miss rate by only a few points => ~1.1-1.4x. The design conflates the general-50%-retire operating point (footprint win, tiny throughput) with an ESFT-style 12%-domain-shard point (big on-domain throughput, off-domain collapse) — it cannot have both "92-97% general retention" AND "2-3x throughput" simultaneously.

**Correctness Concerns:** The 8 named risks are well-chosen (frequency!=utility, super-expert catastrophe, calibration-domain conditioning, silent long-tail loss, prefill-over-predicts, R1 shared-expert overload, MTP/renorm interaction, R3 closed-set-only). Missing/underweighted: (a) THROUGHPUT-MECHANISM ERROR — at fixed k=8, retirement does not reduce per-token I/O; the whole "inverse-linear in bytes read applied to the retired fraction" reasoning is wrong and should be replaced by a measured hit-rate model. (b) The fallback ladder is INVERTED against its own primary citation: REAP's central result is that clean drop+renorm BEATS merging/substitution (Thm 1 functional-subspace-collapse: replacing input-dependent router mixing r(x) with a constant has irreducible error prop Var[r(x)]). R1 (absorb retired gate mass into the always-on shared expert) is exactly that constant convex combination REAP proves inferior — and it just amplifies the shared expert, not reproduce the retired function; R2 (low-rank stub) and R3 (constant kernel value vector) are the same merging flavor. So R1/R2/R3 are DOMINATED by R0, not "more faithful" — the ladder's cheapest-to-most-faithful ordering is backwards. (c) R3's premise is unsound: a cold expert still sees continuous hidden states, not a discrete concept id, so "narrow support => finite table is exact" conflates output-rarity with input-discretizability; keying on concept id re-imports the gating problem and violates the same manifold objection that deferred B3. (d) int4 x prune compounding (above). (e) MTP-acceptance drop at deployment, masked by the DRAFT=0 probe. Only R0 (clean drop) and R4 (external checker, touches no weights) are correctness-defensible.

**Prior Art Overlap:** High overlap; the sound parts are re-application and the novel parts are the weak parts. The prune backbone IS REAP (2510.13999) applied to GLM-5.2 — and REAP already ran GLM-4.5-Air, the SAME lineage — so the efficiency core has near-zero novelty. EASY-EP (2504.06792) already did DeepSeek-family 256-expert domain pruning at 50% retention; STUN, Not-All-Experts (2402.14800), PuzzleMoE cover prune-and-stack; ESFT covers the task-concentrated sub-model. The ladder CONCEPT is HOBBIT/MoE-Infinity's cold-expert precision tiers; R2 (activation-aware low-rank) is CALR/ASVD/SVD-LLM. So R0-R2 are all prior art. Genuinely novel = R1 (shared-expert absorption) and R3 (kernel-tabulated cold tail) — but the pruning-merging facet explicitly reports NO precedent for replacing a trained expert with hand-written deterministic code, AND these are precisely the substitution rungs REAP's merge-collapse theorem argues against. R4 (kernel-checker over pruned-token outputs) has only a Logic-LM analogue, not on pruned models. Net: novelty is concentrated in the refuted/speculative rungs; the defensible content is REAP re-application with the kernel non-load-bearing (the design admits the efficiency win "does NOT require the kernel").

**Strengthened Version:** Reframe as "REAP-on-GLM-5.2-colibri-int4, honestly priced, with the kernel as an optional external checker only." (1) Headline = DISK-GB cut alone: ~47% at 50% retire, deployable as a smaller SSD shard; report throughput as a MEASURED hit-rate delta (expect ~1.1-1.4x on the disk-bound box), never the EASY-EP 2.99x which is a GPU-fit effect that does not transfer. (2) Explicitly separate two operating points and never blend their numbers: general-50%-retire (footprint win, modest throughput, ~90%+ retention to be measured) vs an optional ESFT-style domain-shard at ~12% retention (big on-domain throughput, off-domain collapse, reported as domain-conditioned). (3) Collapse the fallback ladder to R0 (clean drop+renorm — REAP's own best rung) + R4 (kernel-as-external grounding-checker over the pruned model's concept-bearing outputs, the one Law-2-testable kernel role); demote R1/R2/R3 to explicitly-labelled LOSING control arms that test REAP's Thm-1 prediction, not as recommended rungs. (4) Use REAP's BROAD calibration recipe (~1024 general samples), NOT a kernel-concept-narrow set — the kernel MUST NOT define the calibration distribution (it harms general fluency); the kernel earns its place only if R4-as-checker beats kernel-as-text (Law 2). (5) Keep the sound guardrails: super-expert exclusion list first, shared-expert exclusion, long-tail rare-capability probe, generation-side (not prefill) saliency, frequency-null control arm, honesty-first scoring (never PPL alone). (6) Cheapest test: keep the ~$2-3 saliency-flatness gate, but it is necessary-not-sufficient — ADD (i) a hit-rate-vs-union-size measurement to price the ACTUAL throughput, (ii) a small BROAD-suite retention probe on the int4-STACKED model (do not extrapolate REAP's non-int4 number), and (iii) an MTP-on acceptance check at a candidate retention to see whether pruning tanks the draft path. Honest claim ceiling: "REAP retires ~50% of GLM-5.2's routed experts for a ~47% disk-GB cut at retention to-be-measured int4-stacked, a modest hit-rate throughput bump, with the kernel riding only as an optional external checker — the kernel replaces no expert."

---

## Function-Identified Symbolic Replacement (FISR)
**One-liner:** Replace only the small, output-legible minority of GLM-5.2's routed experts with deterministic code / kernel calls, gated by measured fidelity and verified losslessly via the MTP speculative path — treating interpretability's F1~0.6 faithfulness ceiling as a hard constraint that forbids blind replacement and caps coverage to a minority.

**Mechanism:** Three-stage pipeline on the colibri i4i box, reusing the F1-K dump patch (glm52-followup §2.8) and the moe() splice point at glm.c:1270. (1) IDENTIFY — weight-space output-legibility screen: project each routed expert's down-projection columns to vocab via the unembedding (Geva key-value view, interpretability/cheap-approx facets, 2012.14913) and score the entropy/concentration of the induced logit push; low-entropy experts are candidate punctuation/number/format/vocab pushers, high-entropy ones are the illegible general carriers. Flag Super Experts (down_proj massive-activation outliers, pruning-merging facet 2507.23279) onto a hard EXCLUSION list. (2) CHARACTERIZE — dump (routed-input hidden state, gate weight, expert output) triples on a token-class-stratified corpus (punctuation, brackets, digits, indentation, induction-repeats, prose, code/JSON) and fit a fixed surrogate menu per candidate: copy/induction -> deterministic copy of a prior token value; positional -> fixed per-position offset; punctuation/format -> lookup keyed on preceding-token class; number/counting -> rules-engine arithmetic; n-gram -> hashed n-gram->logit table (interpretability facet, Nguyen 2407.12034); measure held-out output-fidelity. (3) CAUSAL GATE — k-sparse probing + causal DLA (what-experts-do facet, The Expert Strikes Back 2604.02178: 80% of labelled experts rank top-8 on matched prompts, unrouted on controls) confirms the expert's contribution IS the described function causally; only experts clearing all three become targets. Substitution has two regimes, both GLM-5.2-specific: LOSSLESS mode runs the symbolic-expert forward (skipping those experts' 19 MB disk reads) as a cheaper DRAFTER whose tokens the full-native forward verifies through the same MTP/grammar-draft machinery colibri already exposes (39-59% MTP acceptance, ~1.0 for grammar-forced spans) — output distribution is provably unchanged, wrong surrogates just cost the I/O they tried to save; FORCED mode hard-replaces the expert output only inside engine-emitted GBNF spans (JSON/brackets/numeric fields), accepting a bounded, targeted correctness change where a deterministic bracket-closer/counter is strictly more reliable than the model. Note MTP dependency: with DRAFT=0 the MTP head is off and LOSSLESS mode must run its own draft loop; with MTP on, FISR composes with native speculation.

**Target Experts:** The function-legible minority, NOT the general carriers. GLM-5.2's own probe measured 10,591 of 16,483 ever-active (layer,expert) cells as UNIVERSAL (fire on all 24 prompts) carrying 93.92% of routing mass, leaving ~5,892 conditional cells at ~6% mass [MEASURED: probe routing-analysis-v2.json]. Two rival target hypotheses the identify-screen adjudicates: (H-tail) legible format/operational experts live in the low-mass conditional tail — matching ST-MoE's "handful of token-class experts per layer" and The Expert Strikes Back's "hundreds across 12 models, many polysemantic" (what-experts-do facet); or (H-core) some universal-core experts ARE high-mass punctuation/whitespace/syntax pushers (fire on every prompt because every prompt has punctuation) and are simultaneously legible AND load-bearing. Identification is by the 3-stage screen above (weight-space entropy -> activation fidelity -> causal DLA), never by frequency (pruning-merging facet: frequency-pruning collapses Qwen3-Coder to 0.011). Layer priors: GLM-5.2's first 3 layers are dense (no experts); ST-MoE puts token-class/format experts SHALLOW (early MoE layers 3-20) and decoder experts are markedly LESS specialized than encoder experts — GLM-5.2 is decoder-only, the single most important honest caveat; OLMoE shows late layers route on the predicted-output token, so late-layer targets are vocabulary/output experts not input-format ones. Hard exclusions: Super Experts and any expert whose surrogate fails the causal gate.

**Expected Yield:** Two separable axes; efficiency is weak, correctness is the real (unmeasured) prize. EFFICIENCY [STIPULATED-contingent, grounded ceiling]: the Amdahl ceiling on the probe box is 5x (disk share 0.8; throughput x1/(0.2+0.8(1-r)) in miss-byte fraction r eliminated) [MEASURED: F1-K §1.3]. But if legible experts are the low-mass tail (H-tail) they carry ~6% of loads -> r<=0.06 -> ~1.05x, negligible; only if they are high-mass (H-core) or on structured code/JSON workloads where format/operational experts fire densely does r reach ~0.1-0.3 -> 1.1-1.4x. Either way this is BELOW REAP's training-free 50%-removal-at-~97%-retention (pruning-merging facet, 2510.13999, GLM-family in-scope) — FISR does NOT win the compression race and must not claim to. SIZE: the replaced-and-deleted fraction shrinks the 370 GB estate linearly, but only by the legible fraction (STIPULATED band 3-15% of the conditional tail from ST-MoE handful-per-layer + Expert-Strikes-Back hundreds-across-12-models -> ~11-55 GB), again under REAP. RETENTION: LOSSLESS mode = 100% by construction (rejection sampling); FORCED mode = a bounded, targeted correctness GAIN on structured spans (balanced brackets, valid JSON, exact counts) that pruning/INT4 cannot provide — a correctness-thesis number, not a compute number, and entirely unmeasured. Honest net: expected_yield as accuracy x compute is roughly 1.0 x [1.01-1.3] STIPULATED, dominated by a correctness guarantee whose magnitude is unknown; the defensible pitch is "correctness-preserving substitution," not "shrink GLM-5.2."

**Correctness Risk:** High and central; the interpretability facet is the binding constraint. (1) FALSE LEGIBILITY: rigorous neuron/expert explanations hit F1~0.6 with "little to no causal efficacy," and precision-0.5 explanations can score perfectly under auto-interp (2309.10312) — an expert that LOOKS like a bracket-closer may be polysemantic, so a label is never a compilable spec. Mitigation: the causal-DLA gate (stage 3) plus fidelity threshold, and crucially LOSSLESS mode makes a wrong surrogate a no-op (it costs the saved I/O, never corrupts output). (2) SUPER EXPERTS: replacing a massive-activation carrier degenerates the model (2507.23279, 3/6144 in Qwen3) — hard exclusion list, data-agnostic down_proj-outlier detection required. (3) OFF-GATE LEAKAGE / IRREDUCIBLE RESIDUAL: even a genuine format expert emits small off-format contributions; shallow deterministic rules absorb only 68-79% of top-1 next-token behavior (Nguyen 2407.12034), the 21-32% residual is exactly the hard part — FORCED mode carries this error, LOSSLESS mode exports it to acceptance rate. (4) CALIBRATION-DOMAIN CONDITIONING: fidelity gates measured on one corpus fail on another (REAP: c4-calibrated pruning of coding models -> incoherent) — scope FORCED replacement to the structured workloads where the gate was built. (5) DECODER PENALTY: ST-MoE found decoder experts far less specialized than encoder experts; GLM-5.2 is decoder-only, so the legible fraction may be small or empty. The design's core risk-control move is to route ALL replacement through speculative verification (LOSSLESS default), spending correctness risk only inside grammar-bounded FORCED spans.

**Kernel Connection:** PARTIAL and honestly bounded — most of FISR is kernel-free. The bulk of legible experts (copy, positional, punctuation, format, generic n-gram) are token/syntax objects the NSM kernel has nothing to say about; their surrogates are plain deterministic code. The KoT concept-kernel / rules-engine attaches at exactly three narrow points: (a) as the SYMBOLIC SOURCE for the operational/semantic tier — where a legible expert implements a concept operation the engine grounds (number/counting -> RULES-1 arithmetic; kinship-relation or canonical-rendering -> engine closure), the surrogate is an engine call not a hand rule; this is the F1-K "kernel-as-expert" seam (glm-kernel-grounding facet D5) but restricted to FUNCTION-identified rather than concept-gated experts, which is cleaner because the gated input set is narrower; (b) as the FIDELITY/GROUNDING CHECKER over FORCED spans (Law 3; Logic-LM +39% analogue); (c) as the GBNF GRAMMAR SOURCE defining where FORCED replacement is provably safe. The honest caveat: the semantic/operational experts the kernel could serve are precisely the ones the interpretability facet says are mid-layer polysemantic and least legible (D5 blocker: an expert is a nonlinear map over a continuous manifold, exact only on a closed gated input set) — so the kernel tier is the most speculative rung, and Law-1 requires the same Gate-0 amendment F1-K needs before any kernel vector enters activations.

**Cheapest Test:** Two-stage, both far below the F1-K/probe ceiling and reusing existing infra. STAGE 0 (near-$0, weight-space, no corpus, no forward pass): stream the int4 expert weights already on the probe box, project each routed expert's down_proj to vocab via the unembedding, and plot the per-expert output-entropy distribution. FALSIFIER: if there is NO low-entropy tail (every expert pushes a broad vocab), then no format/number/punctuation pusher exists and FISR is dead for the cost of one streaming pass over weights already on disk — this is the single cheapest kill and it runs before any model inference. STAGE 1 (~$30-80, one instance-day on the i4i box IF stage 0 finds a tail): activation-dump the low-entropy candidates on a token-class-stratified corpus using the F1-K dump patch, fit the surrogate menu, and measure held-out output-fidelity + causal-DLA null-on-controls. FALSIFIER: if the best-menu fidelity for every candidate sits at the F1~0.6 illegibility floor (2309.10312), no expert is function-identifiable at useful fidelity and FISR is dead before any replacement is built or any speculative loop is written. Only if a legible, causally-confirmed, high-mass-or-structured-workload subset survives both stages does the (larger) LOSSLESS-mode acceptance-rate experiment become worth funding.

**Prior Art:** Novel as a COMPOSITION, re-application at every rung. Closest published: Transcoders (interpretability facet, 2406.11944, NeurIPS'24) replace a whole MLP sublayer with a LEARNED sparse interpretable surrogate at SAE parity — FISR differs by using hand-written/kernel surrogates per-expert, not a learned per-sublayer one. Monet (2412.04139) builds 262k monosemantic experts by construction and deletes groups — architecture-designed, not post-hoc on a trained model like FISR. N-gram rulesets (2407.12034) literally replace next-token behavior deterministically at 68-79% top-1 — FISR's n-gram rung is a direct re-application at the expert granularity. The Expert Strikes Back (2604.02178) supplies the IDENTIFY methodology (k-sparse probing + causal DLA for fine-grained task experts) which FISR consumes and extends to replacement. Speculative decoding / colibri's grammar-forced drafts + MTP supply the LOSSLESS verification machinery. REAP/STUN/NAEE (pruning-merging facet) are the training-free baselines FISR must beat, not prior art for it. The interpretability facet records the decisive gap explicitly: NO published work replaces a trained LLM MoE expert with hand-written deterministic code at preserved perplexity (absence-of-evidence, [search 2026-07-17]) — so FISR-as-a-whole is genuinely novel while every component is borrowed.

**Epistemic Tag:** PLAUSIBLE for the copy/positional/format/n-gram rungs and the LOSSLESS-verification regime; SPECULATIVE for the kernel/semantic-operational rung; with two ESTABLISHED constraints bounding the whole. Established: (i) some token-class/format/operational experts exist and are partially legible (ST-MoE, The Expert Strikes Back — but ESTABLISHED for encoders, only claimed-single-paper for decoder MoEs like GLM-5.2); (ii) explanation faithfulness is poor (F1~0.6, 2309.10312), which is why FISR is architected to REQUIRE a causal fidelity gate and route replacement through lossless speculation rather than trust labels. Plausible-not-established: that GLM-5.2's decoder experts contain a legible subset large enough or high-mass enough to matter — unmeasured, and the decoder penalty + the probe's 93.92% universal-mass concentration are both warning signs. Speculative: that the kernel/engine can serve as the symbolic source for semantic experts, since those are exactly the polysemantic mid-layer experts the faithfulness literature says resist clean specification. Net honest read: FISR is a correctness-preserving-substitution bet whose efficiency upside is marginal and below pruning baselines, whose central open question (does a legible x load-bearing expert subset exist in this decoder model) is answerable for ~$0-80, and whose primary scientific value is a clean falsifier for "are GLM-5.2 expert functions legible enough to compile" — the honest answer to which, on current evidence, leans no.

### Adversarial critique — verdict: **MARGINAL**

**Feasibility Glm52:** The IDENTIFY/CHARACTERIZE screen is cheap and buildable (reuses the F1-K dump patch + moe() splice at glm.c:1270), so the FALSIFIER runs. But the SUBSTITUTION mechanism is largely infeasible in the exact stated config. (1) MTP/DRAFT=0: the design leans on "colibri's existing speculative-decode path" for lossless verification, but the target config is DRAFT=0 = MTP OFF, and the repo states int4 → 0% MTP acceptance. So there is NO existing lossless verify loop to reuse — FISR must build a self-speculative rejection-sampling loop from scratch (net-new, not "existing machinery"). The only path colibri actually exposes in this config is GBNF grammar-FORCING (~1.0 acceptance), which is the forced/lossy mode, not distribution-preserving verification. The advertised "39–59% MTP acceptance" is the int8 MTP head drafting, and does not transfer to a full-minus-symbolic-experts drafter. (2) int4 IDENTIFY noise: the Stage-0 logit-lens entropy screen (project down_proj → vocab via unembedding) is only methodologically valid for the last few layers — the residual stream is not in the output basis at layers 3–40, so the "single cheapest kill" is a valid screen only for late-layer output-vocab experts, and int4 quantization of down_proj adds noise to the entropy estimate. A Stage-0 null does NOT falsify FISR (early/mid experts are invisible to the unembedding); a positive only flags late experts, which OLMoE says route on the predicted-output token. (3) Shared expert: GLM-5.2's always-on shared expert is loaded every token and correctly excluded — but that means it is a fixed I/O floor FISR cannot touch, capping reachable mass. (4) Super-Expert exclusion needs int4-robust down_proj-outlier detection, but the 3/6144 finding is Qwen3-specific and int4 grouped quantization often already special-cases outlier channels, so transfer of the detector is unvalidated; a false negative degenerates the model.

**Yield Realism:** Efficiency yield is not just weak — the LOSSLESS number is close to incoherent, and the design imports the wrong Amdahl figures. The r-fraction / 1.1–1.4x numbers come from F1-K OUTRIGHT-SKIP economics (delete the expert, run the reduced model as THE model → output changes). That is pruning, and it is dominated by REAP's training-free 50% expert removal at ~92–97% retention (GLM-4.5-Air ~92.6%; EASY-EP 2.99x throughput at 50% retain, same DeepSeek/256-expert family). LOSSLESS mode CANNOT claim the r-fraction saving at all: the verify forward runs the FULL model and, per the repo, loads "the union of their expert sets once per layer" — including the symbolic experts you skipped in the draft. So the I/O saved in the draft is re-paid in verify. Working the speculative-decode arithmetic: per accepted block, cost ≈ γ·(full−sym) draft + 1·full verify producing ~α·γ tokens; this only beats baseline when α≈1 AND γ large, asymptoting to (full−sym)/full ≈ ≤1.06x, i.e. the ≤6% symbolic-mass fraction — and it is a net LOSS at small γ or moderate α because you added a near-full extra draft pass. Worse, this ≤1.06x is strictly inferior to simply enabling the int8 MTP head (DRAFT>0 → 2.2–2.8 tok/forward), which uses a far cheaper drafter than full-minus-sym and has nothing to do with FISR. So the honest lossless efficiency is ~1.0–1.06x, fragile, and below both REAP AND the do-nothing MTP baseline. The SIZE claim (11–55 GB from a 3–15% legible fraction) is also optimistic: those ST-MoE/Expert-Strikes-Back "handful per layer / hundreds across 12 models" counts are encoder-biased, GLM-5.2 is decoder-only (the design's own honest caveat), and after the F1~0.6 faithfulness gate the legible×load-bearing×high-fidelity intersection could approach empty. The probe's 93.92% universal-mass concentration is a warning that the low-mass conditional tail (~6%) is where legibility lives, so efficiency reachable-mass is capped near-zero.

**Correctness Concerns:** Central and partly self-defeating. (1) LOSSLESS-mode incoherence (above): you cannot simultaneously have "output distribution provably unchanged" AND "skipping those experts' disk reads" as a net saving — verify re-reads them. The mode's REAL value is safety (a wrong surrogate degrades to a no-op that costs I/O), not speed; the design oversells it as an efficiency mechanism. (2) FORCED mode is redundant with, and more dangerous than, constrained decoding: colibri already GBNF-forces valid JSON/brackets at ~1.0 acceptance at the OUTPUT layer with zero weight surgery. Replacing a mid-network expert's residual contribution to enforce structural validity is a high-variance, indirect lever for something the output layer controls directly — it adds Super-Expert risk and off-gate-leakage risk for NO structural guarantee grammar doesn't already give. The only thing grammar can't do is pick the RIGHT value (counting/arithmetic), but that too is best done by logit-biasing an engine-computed token at the output, not by mid-stack activation surgery. (3) False legibility: F1~0.6 explanation faithfulness with "little to no causal efficacy" means a bracket-closer label is not a compilable spec; the causal-DLA gate mitigates but the design correctly treats this as a hard coverage cap. (4) The n-gram rung mis-transfers Nguyen's 68–79%: that is a WHOLE-MODEL next-token statistic, not a single-expert-contribution statistic — an expert emits a residual delta, so the 68–79% figure does not bound surrogate fidelity for an expert. (5) Calibration-domain conditioning (REAP): fidelity gates built on one token-class corpus fail on another. Net: the causal-gate discipline and lossless-safety wrapper are sound hygiene, but the substitution seam is the wrong seam for correctness.

**Prior Art Overlap:** Not refuted, but boxed in on every axis by cheaper existing methods. Efficiency is dominated by REAP/STUN/EASY-EP training-free pruning (50%/40% removal, GLM-family in scope, already near-lossless + INT4) — the design admits this. The FORCED structural-correctness win is subsumed by GBNF constrained decoding, which colibri already ships. The value-correctness win is the Logic-LM +39% / D7 output-seam rung the programme already privileges. Transcoders (2406.11944) and Monet (2412.04139) are the learned in-network analogues; FISR's hand-written/kernel surrogate is a genuine differentiator, but the interpretability facet's absence-of-evidence null (no published work replaces a trained MoE expert with hand-written code at preserved perplexity) is real and unbroken — which cuts both ways: novel, but also no existence proof that the legible fraction survives to useful fidelity in a decoder model. The composition is novel; every rung is individually dominated or redundant.

**Strengthened Version:** Retreat from "system" to "instrument + redirection," which is where the design's own epistemic tag already points. KEEP: (A) FISR-IDENTIFY as a pure ~$0–80 FALSIFIER — the single scientific deliverable is a clean yes/no + size on "does GLM-5.2's decoder MoE contain a legible × causally-confirmed × high-fidelity routed-expert subset?" Fix Stage-0 to late-layer-only logit-lens (the unembedding projection is invalid earlier) plus causal-DLA null-on-controls; report the fraction and its fidelity distribution against the F1~0.6 floor. This is valuable regardless of outcome and is the honest core. (B) Keep the LOSSLESS wrapper ONLY as a correctness-safety guarantee (wrong surrogate = no-op), never as an efficiency claim; drop the r-fraction/1.1–1.4x import entirely and state efficiency as ~1.0x, below REAP and below just enabling MTP. DROP: the in-network SUBSTITUTION system as an efficiency play (dominated by REAP) and FORCED expert-replacement for structural validity (dominated by GBNF constrained decoding). REDIRECT the correctness thesis off the expert seam onto the OUTPUT/logits seam: GBNF for structure + kernel/rules-engine as a verified output-token oracle via logit-bias or draft-injection (the D7 / Logic-LM +39% rung), which delivers the "correctness-preserving substitution" goal without weight surgery, Super-Expert risk, or faithfulness-ceiling exposure. The kernel attaches only there. Net defensible pitch: FISR is (1) a cheap honest experiment answering whether GLM-5.2 expert functions are compilable — on current evidence (decoder penalty + 93.92% universal-mass concentration + F1~0.6) the answer leans NO — and (2) a demonstration that if you want deterministic correctness, the intervention belongs at the output boundary, not inside the MoE sum. Both are worth doing; the proposed expert-substitution engine is not.

---

## Kernel-Certified Expert Substitution (KCES)
**One-liner:** Don't replace experts wholesale — put a cheap kernel concept-similarity + output-entropy GATE in front of every candidate expert; where it certifies (operational/token-class experts, shared-expert co-processing), run a deterministic kernel transform instead of the ~19MB int4 load+matmul; where it can't (mid-layer polysemantic experts), fall through to the real expert untouched.

**Mechanism:** A single gate mechanism, applied per (expert, token, layer), with four substitution rungs of increasing aggression. GLM-5.2 geometry: layers 1-3 are dense (first_k_dense_replace=3), so KCES lives in the 75 MoE layers (256 routed + shared expert, top-8), DRAFT=0 greedy so every arm is byte-reproducible for KL scoring. THE GATE (the concrete kernel role): offline, harvest each candidate expert's "concept signature" = mean KoT-encoder concept vector of the contexts on which it fires (from F1-K's existing MoE-input activation dumps + REAP-style 1,024-sample calibration). At inference, encode the token's context to a concept vector; the gate FIRES iff (a) cosine to the expert signature clears a frozen threshold AND (b) that signature's calibrated output-distribution entropy/effective-rank is low. RUNGS: R0 grounding-veto (no substitution): the rules-engine reads the MoE-output-induced logits on kernel-adjudicable tokens and attenuates/vetoes expert mass pushing an engine-illicit token (Logic-LM's engine-owns-correctness moved from the text gateway to the MoE-internal seam) — pure correctness, zero expert removed. R1 operational-expert substitution: for gate-certified low-entropy experts (bracket-closing/punctuation/numeric-format/script experts), skip the expert load and add a deterministic surrogate (calibrated lookup / rank-1 / kernel concept-transform) in its routed slot at the router's own weight — an I/O + FLOP saving on gated (token,layer) slots, with the router mixing r(x) left fully live. R2 shared-expert co-processor: run a kernel-grounded correction term alongside GLM-5.2's always-on shared expert (never replacing it) — the shared expert is the highest-leverage common-knowledge object but is un-tabulatable, so co-process only. R3 concept-shard graceful drop: for an ESFT/EASY-EP-identified concept subset, replace the dropped-half experts with a kernel deterministic fallback keyed on concept rather than zeroing them. Crucially the gate certifies on CONDITIONAL IMPACT (similarity+entropy), never frequency, and never replaces r(x) with a constant — the two design choices that dodge the frequency-prune and merge-collapse nulls below.

**Target Experts:** Not "domain experts" (that folk theory is the Mixtral null [what-experts-do: 2401.04088 — no topic specialization]). KCES targets the three measurably-legible classes: (1) OPERATIONAL / token-class routed experts — bracket-closing/punctuation/numeric-format/script experts documented by ST-MoE and "The Expert Strikes Back" (80% of labelled operational experts rank top-8 on matched prompts, unrouted on controls) [what-experts-do: 2202.08906, 2604.02178]; in GLM-5.2 these concentrate in the FIRST and LAST MoE layers per Mixtral's indentation/"self"-pinning finding, so R1 is applied there. Identified by: offline output-entropy/effective-rank histogram per (expert, calibration-cluster) over F1-K's already-collected MoE-input dumps, then k-sparse probing + causal DLA (Expert-Strikes-Back method), then kernel concept-signature match. (2) The ~75 always-on SHARED experts — DeepSeekMoE shows they carry "fundamental knowledge" (removal = +33.5% Pile loss) [what-experts-do: 2401.06066]; R2 co-processes, does not replace. (3) A concept-conditioned routed SUBSET for R3, identified ESFT/EASY-EP-style (~5-15% of experts cover a task; retain-half → 2.99x throughput on DeepSeek-family) [pruning-merging: 2407.01906; glm-kernel-grounding: 2504.06792]. HARD EXCLUSIONS: super-experts (3-of-6144 down_proj outlier carriers whose removal degenerates the model) [pruning-merging: 2507.23279] and long-tail experts (rare-activation experts encode indispensable info) [pruning-merging: 2604.23036]; and late-layer experts that route on the ABOUT-TO-BE-PREDICTED token, not the input concept [what-experts-do/cheap-approximation: 2409.02060, 2604.09780] — an input-concept gate structurally mispredicts these, so R1/R3 are confined to early/mid MoE layers or must move the gate to generation-side validation.

**Expected Yield:** GROUNDED ceiling-shapes (published, verified): REAP conditional-saliency pruning at 50% expert removal retains ~92.6% on the closest GLM-family datum (GLM-4.5-Air coding 0.553 vs 0.597, training-free, calibration-only) and ~97.6% on Qwen3-Coder [pruning-merging: 2510.13999, full-text]; EASY-EP retain-half → comparable quality at 2.99x throughput on DeepSeek-family [glm-kernel-grounding: 2504.06792]; HOBBIT quantizing <20% of (cold) experts to INT4/INT2 costs <1% accuracy [pruning-merging: 2411.01433]; deterministic shallow rules absorb 68-79% of transformer top-1 next-token predictions [interpretability: 2407.12034]. On the disk-bound probe box, throughput is inverse-linear in expert-bytes-read with a 5.0x Amdahl ceiling (d=0.8) [repo followup §1.3], so if the gate fires on ~15-20% of (token,layer) expert slots (HOBBIT-safe-fraction as proxy) with a deterministic surrogate, that is ~1.1-1.25x decode speedup PLUS the R3 disk-estate cut toward f·370GB. STIPULATED (unmeasured, this family's open delta): that a gate-conditioned deterministic/kernel SUBSTITUTE beats simply PRUNING the same expert on the gate-fired subset (KL(real‖surrogate) < KL(real‖zeroed)), and that a KERNEL concept-gate identifies the legible subset better than a kernel-free activation-cluster gate. R0's correctness yield is NOT +18-39% — that is Logic-LM's TEXT-seam number and is the null to beat, not a claim (see correctness_risk). Net honest target: match REAP-50% quality (≥92% GLM-family) at a modest, physics-bounded I/O win — STIPULATED that the kernel earns any increment over the kernel-free lookup/pruning baseline.

**Correctness Risk:** HIGH and concentrated in the gate. (1) Gate false-accept is the single point of failure: substituting a deterministic value where the expert actually mattered emits a wrong token; the gate must be near-perfect, and unlike pinning (byte-identical, quality-safe) this rung CHANGES the computed function. (2) Explanation-faithfulness null: rigorously-evaluated expert/neuron labels hit F1~0.6 with "little to no causal efficacy" — a legible-sounding label is NOT a compilable spec, so surrogates must be validated purely by held-out output-distribution KL, never by label plausibility [interpretability: 2309.10312]. (3) Merge-collapse is a PROVEN failure mode: replacing input-dependent router mixing r(x) with a constant convex combination gives irreducible error ∝ Var[r(x)]·expert-gap [pruning-merging: 2510.13999 Thm 1] — KCES only dodges it by keeping r(x) live and substituting the VALUE conditionally; any drift toward averaging experts re-incurs it. (4) Frequency-based removal collapses catastrophically (50% frequency-pruned Qwen3-Coder scores 0.011) [pruning-merging: 2510.13999 Table 3] — the gate MUST certify on conditional impact, never activation frequency. (5) Late-layer output-token routing → input-concept gate mispredicts → confine to early/mid layers. (6) Calibration-domain sensitivity: c4-calibrated pruning of coding models produces incoherent output — the gate is calibration-domain-conditioned, so "near-lossless" is per-domain only. (7) The brutal bar: every rung must beat kernel-as-text (Law 2) AND REAP-pruned+INT4 at matched disk+FLOPs; the programme's runtime-judgment ledger is 0-for-5, so the modal outcome is a tie where a kernel-free lookup/pruning does as well.

**Kernel Connection:** Concrete and load-bearing at the GATE, honestly weak at the VALUE. WHERE IT ATTACHES: (a) the gate's similarity function IS a KoT-encoder operation — NSM-grounded deterministic explication→concept-vector→cosine against a per-expert concept signature; this is the encoder's native output used as a certifier, not injected into activations (so R0 and the gate respect Law-1; only R1-R3 substitution writes to the residual stream and inherits F1-K's MAINTAINER GATE 0 Law-1 amendment). (b) R0/R2 use the RULES-ENGINE (RULES-1 forward chainer, certified 858/858 vs CLUTRR gold) to adjudicate factual tokens and supply the veto/correction — the direct descendant of Logic-LM's engine-owns-correctness. (c) The genuinely kernel-SPECIFIC bet (the only thing a per-expert lookup cannot do): the engine predicts the surrogate value for concept COMBINATIONS never seen in calibration via entailment closure — a compositional generalization a frozen lookup table structurally lacks. WHERE IT DOES NOT ATTACH: mid-layer polysemantic routed experts have no clean concept signature (the interpretability null) → the gate simply never fires there, which is the SAFE failure mode (fall through to the real expert), turning the null from a defeater into a designed-in guardrail. And the R1 deterministic-VALUE claim must beat a calibrated kernel-free lookup surrogate (the knull deflator that capped the programme 4x) — expect the value to be structure-free unless the compositional-entailment bet pays.

**Cheapest Test:** Two tiers, both cheap. TIER 0 (no GPU, no training, CPU-minutes over ALREADY-COLLECTED bytes): F1-K already dumps MoE-input activations at gated positions. For each routed expert in a few early/mid GLM-5.2 MoE layers, compute the output-distribution entropy / effective-rank on its calibration input cluster. FALSIFIER for the whole substitution premise: if NO expert exhibits a low-entropy near-deterministic output cluster (all high-entropy over their gated inputs), Rungs 1/3 are dead before a single surrogate is fit — the interpretability null wins at GLM-5.2's 256-expert scale, and only R0 (grounding-veto) survives. TIER 1 (single GPU, no training, ONE expert — runnable on a DeepSeek-MoE-16B or Qwen3-30B-A3B proxy to avoid the 370GB GLM-5.2 estate): pick one gate-identified operational expert (bracket/punctuation, via activation clustering on REAP's 1,024-sample set), fit a trivial deterministic surrogate (majority-value / rank-1 / lookup) on its gated cluster, and on held-out tokens measure KL(real-expert-logits ‖ surrogate) vs KL(real ‖ zeroed/pruned). FALSIFIER: if surrogate-KL is not materially below prune-KL on the gate-fired subset, KCES adds nothing over REAP pruning — kill R1/R3. KERNEL-SPECIFIC falsifier (same run): if a KoT concept-similarity gate does not select the low-entropy subset better than a plain activation-cluster gate, the kernel earns nothing here (fall back to kernel-free) — the modal expectation.

**Prior Art:** Closest published: transcoders (2406.11944, NeurIPS'24) — a LEARNED wide-sparse surrogate replacing an MLP sublayer at SAE-parity; KCES's R1 is the deterministic/kernel-authored, gated analogue. REAP (2510.13999) — conditional-saliency (gate-value × activation-norm) one-shot pruning at 50%; KCES borrows its "certify on conditional impact, not frequency" principle and its calibration protocol, but SUBSTITUTES rather than deletes. HOBBIT (2411.01433) — per-expert precision ladder for cold experts; a deterministic/kernel stub is the extreme low rung of exactly that ladder. Monet (2412.04139) — delete monosemantic expert groups (legible-by-construction MoE). SteerMoE (2509.09660) — training-free router-logit expert (de)activation, the intervention primitive R0's veto uses. Logic-LM (2305.12295) — engine-owns-correctness at the text seam, R0's direct analogue and its ceiling/null. Geva key-value-memory + Memory-Layers-at-Scale (2012.14913, 2412.09764) — the expert-as-addressable-lookup license (but TRAINED, not hand-written).
