NOVELTY: an explicit facet search returned a NULL for replacing a trained production-MoE expert with hand-written deterministic code [interpretability + cheap-approximation facets, 2026-07-17]. KCES is therefore a novel SYNTHESIS — REAP's conditional-saliency + transcoder-style surrogacy + a SYMBOLIC concept-kernel gate (not a learned activation threshold) + a deterministic/kernel surrogate (not a learned one) — with two genuinely open claims (gated-substitution beats pruning; kernel-gate beats activation-gate); the individual primitives are re-applications.

**Epistemic Tag:** PLAUSIBLE-to-SPECULATIVE, tagged per rung. R0 grounding-veto: established MECHANISM (Logic-LM), but its win over the kernel-as-text null is SPECULATIVE (programme 0-for-5 on runtime-judgment interfaces). R1 operational substitution: rests on ESTABLISHED facts — operational/token-class experts demonstrably exist and are legible (ST-MoE, Expert-Strikes-Back), deterministic shallow rules absorb 68-79% of next-token behavior (N-gram), conditional-impact pruning retains ~92% at 50% on GLM-family (REAP) — but the load-bearing claim (a gated deterministic/kernel surrogate beats pruning on the certified subset, AND the kernel gate beats a kernel-free activation gate) is SPECULATIVE with an explicit no-prior-art search null. R2/R3: SPECULATIVE (shared-expert substitution is fundamentally blocked, so R2 is co-processing; R3's substitute-vs-drop delta is unmeasured). WHY not higher: the interpretability null (experts aren't concept-shaped) and the explanation-faithfulness null (labels aren't specs) both bite directly, and the programme's own four content-not-structure deflations predict the kernel ties a kernel-free lookup at the value seam. WHY not lower: the design is falsifiable for near-zero cost (Tier-0 over existing bytes), fails SAFE (gate-miss = real expert runs), and structurally dodges the two proven collapse modes (frequency-prune, merge) by construction — which is more than most rungs in the seam map can say.

### Adversarial critique — verdict: **MARGINAL**

**Feasibility Glm52:** Partially infeasible AS WRITTEN on GLM-5.2 colibri int4, for three concrete mechanism reasons the design under-states. (1) THE GATE IS NOT A KERNEL OPERATION AT RUNTIME. The gate needs a per-(token,layer) concept vector to cosine against an expert signature. The KoT encoder is a deterministic explication->vector pipeline over TEXT; inside a mid-stack MoE layer there is only a 6144-dim residual-stream hidden state, not text. You cannot run the NSM explication encoder on a hidden state. F1-K already hit this wall: its runnable per-token gate (G-emb, glm52-followup §2.3) is "cosine of the HIDDEN STATE against per-concept ANCHOR vectors (mean HIDDEN STATES harvested from renderings)" — i.e. hidden-space cosine, which is exactly the kernel-FREE activation-cluster gate KCES itself names as the modal-loss baseline. The kernel-specific concept-space gate is only computable per-layer via a LEARNED hidden->concept projection, which forfeits training-free and re-triggers the Law-1 amendment (MAINTAINER GATE 0). So the moment the gate is made runnable, it stops being kernel. (2) R0 CANNOT ACT AT THE MoE-INTERNAL SEAM. R0 wants to attenuate "expert mass pushing an engine-illicit token" by reading "MoE-output-induced logits." But a layer-l MoE output is a residual vector; logits exist only after all 78 layers + unembed. Knowing which token a layer-l expert pushes requires a logit-lens projection through the remaining ~30+ layers — approximate and not what colibri computes. R0 at the true logit seam is just the existing text-gateway grounding-checker (the d3-text / Logic-LM ceiling already in the ledger), not a new internal seam. (3) COLD-LOAD ECONOMICS DON'T CLOSE. On the disk-bound i4i box (d=0.8, 11.4 GB/token, 5.0x Amdahl ceiling) the I/O win exists only for COLD expert misses; operational/token-class experts (bracket/punctuation/indentation) are HIGH-frequency, so they are almost always already pinned/hot (Mixtral P(reuse)>0.46) — skipping their LOAD saves ~nothing. And the current F1-K run is on a CPU-bound GCP EPYC VM, not the disk-bound box, where the gate's own compute is a net COST. Geometry itself is fine and helps: first_k_dense_replace=3 confines KCES to layers 4-78, DRAFT=0 (MTP off) makes every arm byte-reproducible for KL scoring — but note DRAFT=0 also forfeits MTP's k-token-per-load amortization, so the 1.1-1.25x is measured against an un-amortized baseline. Super-expert exclusion is claimed but NO super-experts have been identified on GLM-5.2 (they are model-specific down_proj outliers, 3-of-6144 on Qwen3) so R1/R3 currently ship with an EMPTY exclusion list — a live degeneration hazard. Finally the cheapest-test premise is factually wrong TODAY: F1-K has not run ("no results-log/f1k.jsonl, no phase:final run"; still stuck in inertness-gate bring-up 2026-07-17), so the "already-collected MoE-input dumps" do not exist — and even when they do, they are moe() INPUTS, so per-expert output-entropy/effective-rank needs an expert-forward recompute (needs the int4 weights = 370GB estate or a proxy), not free CPU-minutes over existing bytes.

**Yield Realism:** The GROUNDED ceilings are cited correctly but the KERNEL increment over them is structurally near-zero, which the "expected_yield" hedge admits but under-weights. The published ceiling for training-free 50% expert removal on the closest GLM-family datum is REAP's GLM-4.5-Air coding 0.553 vs 0.597 = 92.6% retention — and note that is the WEAKEST model in REAP's set (Qwen3-Coder 97.6%), so GLM-family experts are already the harder case. KCES's whole delta is the STIPULATED claim KL(real||surrogate) < KL(real||zeroed) on the gate-fired subset. Two anti-correlations make that delta small exactly where the gate fires: (a) the experts safe to substitute are the LOW-ENTROPY / near-constant-output experts — but a near-constant expert is precisely the one deletion hurts LEAST (low conditional impact = what REAP already keeps quality on), so surrogate-minus-zero is a small difference of two small impacts; (b) low-entropy operational experts are HIGH-frequency, hence never cold-loaded, so the I/O saving is also smallest there. Safe-to-substitute = cheap-to-delete = already-hot: the value win and the speed win both vanish in the same region. So even if both open claims are TRUE, the effect size is plausibly below REAP's own 7.4% GLM retention gap and below run-to-run noise. The 15-20% fire-rate is imported from HOBBIT's <1%-loss-at-<20%-quantized number, which is a category error — HOBBIT still RUNS every expert at INT4/INT2 (approximate function preserved); it never skips or replaces one with deterministic code, so it licenses nothing about substitution fire-rate. Realistic honest yield: KCES's disk/throughput numbers ARE achievable but they equal REAP/EASY-EP (retain-half -> ~2.99x on DeepSeek-family; f*370GB disk cut) and are 100% kernel-free — the kernel earns ~0 increment at the value seam. The programme's own ledger (0-for-5 on runtime-judgment, 4x content-not-structure deflations) is the correct prior: modal outcome is a tie with a kernel-free lookup/pruner.

**Correctness Concerns:** The design's own 7-item correctness list is good; the additional load-bearing failures it misses: (1) PREFILL/CALIBRATION GATE OVER-FIRES AT GENERATION. The gate is calibrated on prefill/calibration clusters, but "Myth of Expert Specialization" (2604.09780) shows prefill routing fingerprints OVER-PREDICT replaceability: pruning "collapsed" experts costs <10% NLL prompt-side but a LARGE hit at generation. A prefill-fit gate therefore has a structural false-ACCEPT bias precisely at generation, the failure direction that emits wrong tokens. (2) CROSS-LAYER / AUTOREGRESSIVE ERROR COMPOUNDING is untested by the proposed test. The Tier-1 falsifier measures ONE expert's KL in isolation on held-out tokens; it cannot see error accumulating across up to 75 MoE layers x multiple gated slots/layer x autoregressive feedback. A per-slot KL below the prune-KL can still compound to incoherent generation — the exact regime REAP's calibration-domain-sensitivity result (c4-calibrated coding model -> incoherent output) demonstrates. (3) EMPTY SUPER-EXPERT EXCLUSION LIST on GLM-5.2 (see feasibility): the guardrail the design relies on does not yet exist for this model. (4) THE LAYER-LOCALIZATION CLAIM IS SELF-CONTRADICTORY: R1 is placed in "FIRST and LAST MoE layers" (Mixtral pinning) but the design ALSO excludes last layers because OLMoE shows they route on the about-to-be-predicted token — so the two lit findings collide and R1's real estate collapses to early/mid layers only, shrinking the addressable slots and voiding the "last-layer operational expert" target. (5) GATE FALSE-ACCEPT IS UNBOUNDED BY LABELS: the explanation-faithfulness null (2309.10312, F1~0.6, "little causal efficacy") means a legible-sounding expert label is not a spec; the design correctly says validate by KL only — but combined with (1)+(2) the held-out KL must be a FULL-GENERATION KL under distribution shift, not the single-expert prefill KL the test proposes. The design DOES structurally dodge the two PROVEN collapse modes (frequency-prune via conditional-impact gate; merge-collapse via keeping r(x) live and never averaging) — that part is sound and is the family's real strength. It also fails SAFE (gate-miss -> real expert runs). But R2 is not correctness-neutral: co-processing ADDS a kernel correction term to the always-on shared expert, which writes to the residual stream, inherits Gate-0 / Law-1, and its lift is entirely unmeasured (F1-K, its parent, has not established that the ADD carrier lifts anything at all).

**Prior Art Overlap:** HIGH overlap on every primitive; genuine novelty confined to two claims the design itself tags likely-to-fail, one of which is mechanism-blocked. Transcoders (2406.11944, NeurIPS'24) already replace an MLP sublayer with a wide-sparse surrogate at SAE parity — KCES R1 is the deterministic/hand-authored analogue, and the deterministic/hand-written part is exactly where the interpretability + cheap-approximation facets returned an explicit NULL (no prior art for replacing a trained production-MoE expert with hand-written code) AND where the lit predicts fundamental failure: N-gram rulesets leave a 21-32% residual (2407.12034) that IS the hard part, plain deterministic SVD/factorization is a known negative (spectra are flat), and the interpretability null says experts aren't concept-shaped. So R1 lives in the precise gap the literature flags as [speculative]/likely-fundamental. The GATE is REAP's conditional saliency (gate-value x activation-norm) recomputed per calibration-cluster and re-badged as a concept certifier; the SUBSTITUTE-vs-DELETE move is the only structural difference from REAP, and it is the small-effect region argued above. R0 is Logic-LM (2305.12295) engine-owns-correctness relocated — but as shown it relocates back to the text/logit seam that is the programme's existing null, not forward to a new seam. R2/R3 are F1-K/EASY-EP/SteerMoE re-applications. NOT refuted outright, but the composition of nulls it must thread is unusually dense (merge-collapse, frequency-prune, SVD-negative, distillation-70%-loss, interpretability, faithfulness, output-token-routing), and the two truly novel bets — (i) gated deterministic/kernel substitution beats pruning on the certified subset, (ii) a kernel concept-gate selects the legible subset better than a kernel-free activation gate — are BOTH the programme's modal-loss shape, and (ii) is not merely unproven but mechanism-blocked (the kernel-space gate is not computable per-layer without a trained bridge; the computable gate is kernel-free). So: novel synthesis, yes; novel WIN, unsupported and structurally improbable.

**Strengthened Version:** Drop expert SUBSTITUTION as the headline (R1/R2/R3-value are marginal-to-dead-end for the reasons above) and keep the two things that survive the critique — one free measurement, one clean single-claim experiment — plus R0 honestly re-scoped. (A) TIER-0 LEGIBILITY MEASUREMENT AS A FIRST-CLASS DELIVERABLE, not a mere falsifier: on a proxy 256-expert MoE (DeepSeek-MoE-16B / Qwen3-30B-A3B, to avoid the 370GB estate and the fact that GLM-5.2 F1-K dumps do not yet exist), run each routed expert on its calibration-cluster INPUTS and histogram the effective-RANK of its output-vector SET over the cluster (drop the ill-defined "output-distribution entropy" — an FFN outputs a vector, not a distribution). This directly measures how many experts are near-tabulatable at GLM-5.2's fine-grained scale and either licenses or kills R1/R3 before any surrogate is fit; it is publishable either way and needs no Law-1 amendment. (B) REFRAME THE KERNEL FROM VALUE TO SELECTOR: do not put the kernel in the residual stream at all. Use the KoT concept coverage as a SALIENCY PRIOR for a REAP-style DELETION pruner and test one falsifiable claim — "concept-coverage-weighted selection retains more quality than REAP's activation-norm saliency at matched sparsity" — deletion-only (fails safe to REAP's known behavior, no new correctness surface, dodges merge/frequency nulls by construction), on the proxy MoE, validated by FULL-GENERATION KL under distribution shift (not single-expert prefill KL). This is the one place the kernel has structure REAP's frequency x norm lacks (compositional concept coverage), it is a clean single novel claim, and it must beat REAP+INT4 at matched disk+FLOPs — the honest bar. (C) KEEP R0 ONLY as an OUTPUT-logit grounding-veto, labelled honestly as the text/logit-seam checker (Logic-LM's seam, not a new MoE-internal one), whose sole job is to beat kernel-as-text — the programme's existing correctness bet, no new claim. Constraints that must ride along: confine any surviving substitution to EARLY/MID MoE layers (resolving the Mixtral-pinning vs OLMoE-output-routing collision); identify GLM-5.2's super-experts FIRST and exclude them; and state up front that the modal expected result is a TIE with the kernel-free pruner at the value seam, so the deliverable is the measurement (A) + the selection test (B), where a null is a real, reportable datum. This version is cheaper, fails safe, makes ONE novel falsifiable claim instead of four speculative ones, and stops asserting a kernel win at a seam where the kernel is provably inert.

---

## Cluster-Distill-to-Lookup (CDL)
**One-liner:** Trade GLM-5.2's ~370 GB streamed-expert estate for a few GB of RAM-resident lookup tables by distilling saliency-selected expert clusters into input-conditioned surrogates, sized by the MoE-approximation literature and validated against REAP-pruning + kernel-as-text.

**Mechanism:** Four rungs, all splicing at colibri moe() (glm.c:1270, after the top-8 weighted sum + shared expert, before residual write-back), all scored by prefill/loglikelihood not generation (ASM-1988; 0.09 tok/s makes generative eval unaffordable), all training-free at inference (the "distillation" is offline calibration; the frozen artifact is a deterministic table/factor, so DRAFT=0 byte-reproducibility is preserved on covered vs uncovered tokens separately).

R1 — Hashed key-value for token-class experts. FFNs are key-value memories [interpretability/cheap-approx facets: Geva 2012.14913]; the low-entropy operational experts (punctuation, bracket-closing, numeric, formatting) have small empirical input sets [what-experts-do facet: ST-MoE 2202.08906, OLMoE 2409.02060, Expert Strikes Back 2604.02178]. Distil each to a LookupFFN-style hashed table [cheap-approx facet 2403.07221]. Ceiling on shallow deterministic rules is the N-gram bound: 79% TinyStories / 68% Wikipedia top-1 match [interpretability facet 2407.12034] — the 21-32% residual bounds R1.

R2 — Activation-aware low-rank per cluster. Cluster the 256 experts/MoE layer (layers 3-77) by REAP-saliency-weighted routing co-activation, distil each cluster to a shared whitened low-rank basis + tiny per-expert corrective [cheap-approx facet: ASVD/SVD-LLM/CALR 2508.16680; MC-SMoE 2310.01334]. HONEST GLM-5.2 CAVEAT: plain SVD is a known negative (spectra flat), and DeepSeek-family from-scratch experts are near-orthogonal (pairwise cosine ~=0) [what-experts-do facet 2406.18219], so cluster redundancy is LOW and this rung is the weakest for GLM-5.2 — I keep it only as the falsification anchor (see cheapest_test).

R3 — Product-key / memory-layer distill (the core bet). Distil a whole saliency-selected cluster, or the always-on shared expert bank, into a product-key memory / PEER-style singleton-expert store initialized from the experts' own key-value structure [cheap-approx/glm-kernel facets: Memory Layers 2412.09764, PEER 2407.04153, UltraMemV2 2508.18756]. Retrieval preserves input-conditioning, which is exactly what dodges the merging-collapse theorem. GLM-5.2 payoff is disk-specific: the model is disk-I/O-bound (80% of wall-time streaming experts), so a few-GB RAM-resident memory store that covers a token fraction converts SSD reads into RAM lookups — attacking the measured bottleneck, not just param count.

R4 — Kernel-seeded value store. The memory layer's value entries are seeded/augmented with KoT per-concept definition offsets built by the F1-K construction (mean hidden-state difference with/without kernel explication prepended), addressed by kernel concept keys behind a trained bridge (Law-1 amendment, Gate 0). Kernel supplies deterministic content; retrieval supplies conditioning.

**Target Experts:** NOT single domain experts (Mixtral topic-null [what-experts-do facet 2401.04088]) and NOT mid-layer polysemantic routed experts (not concept-shaped [interpretability facet]). Identification is REAP-style conditional-impact saliency = gate-value x activation-conditioned expert-norm [pruning-merging facet 2510.13999], NEVER frequency (frequency-pruning collapses GLM-family experts to 0.011 [REAP Table 3]). Concrete targets: (R1) low-activation-entropy token-class/operational experts with small empirical input sets; (R3) whole saliency-ranked clusters, OR — cleanest structurally — the always-on shared expert(s), which carry DeepSeekMoE common knowledge (+33.5% loss when disabled [what-experts-do facet 2401.06066]) and have NO router-mixing variance to collapse, making them the ideal memory-layer target (Memory Layers' biggest gains are on factual/common knowledge). Hard exclusions: super-experts (down_proj massive-activation carriers, data-agnostic model-specific detection; 3/6144 pruned collapsed Qwen3-30B [pruning-merging facet 2507.23279]); late-layer experts (route on the predicted OUTPUT token, so an input-keyed lookup mispredicts structurally [what-experts-do/cheap-approx facets: OLMoE, Myth 2604.09780]) — keep R3 in mid-stack (~layer 40, the F1-K L1 splice layer).

**Expected Yield:** GROUNDED anchors: (baseline to beat) REAP training-free 50% routed-expert removal retains GLM-4.5-Air 0.553 vs 0.597 coding = 92.6% (weakest of its set) [pruning-merging facet 2510.13999, full-text]; (floor that motivates lookup over dense) distilling sparse->FLOP-matched dense keeps only ~30% of the sparse quality gain at 99% size cut [cheap-approx facet, Switch 2101.03961]; (ceiling) trained key-value lookup MATCHES MoE at matched compute+params, biggest gains on factual tasks [cheap-approx/glm-kernel facets 2412.09764]; (shape-only, NON-transferable) MC-SMoE 80% memory / 20% FLOP at <1% degradation but small/discriminative only, and REAP's collapse theorem proves it dies on large-generative [pruning-merging facet 2310.01334 + Thm 1]. STIPULATED target for GLM-5.2: at 50% routed-expert-parameter reduction (370->~185 GB disk) retain >=95% (beating REAP's 92.6%) by keeping input-conditioning via retrieval. Compression->throughput is GROUNDED arithmetic on the measured d=0.8 disk share (F1-K Amdahl): r=0.5 miss-byte reduction -> 1.67x tok/s; a fully RAM-resident surrogate for the covered fraction approaches the r->1, ~5.0x on-box ceiling. The 95% retention is STIPULATED (no GLM-5.2 lookup-retrofit exists; parity numbers are all from-scratch training). Net honest yield: GROUNDED that ~50% expert mass is cheaply removable and that lookup preserves capacity dense cannot; STIPULATED that a distilled retrofit beats REAP+INT4 at matched budget.

**Correctness Risk:** (1) Merging-collapse — FUNDAMENTAL for R2 on GLM-5.2: irreducible error ~ Var[r(x)] x expert-gap [pruning-merging facet, REAP Thm 1], and GLM-5.2's near-orthogonal from-scratch experts [2406.18219] maximize the gap; R3's retrieval is the mitigation, R2 is expected to fail. (2) Super-expert inclusion -> catastrophic degeneration [2507.23279]; requires a data-agnostic exclusion pass first. (3) Calibration-domain conditioning: pruned/distilled models are domain-conditioned; c4-calibration produced incoherent code output [pruning-merging facet, REAP]; the surrogate inherits the calibration corpus's blind spots. (4) Prefill over-predicts replaceability; late-layer output-token routing means input-keyed lookups mispredict — MUST validate on generation-side tokens [what-experts-do/cheap-approx facets: OLMoE, Myth 2604.09780]. (5) Explanation unfaithfulness (F1~0.6, weak causal efficacy [interpretability facet 2309.10312]) forbids HAND-written code — the surrogate must be distilled/calibrated, not authored. (6) Honesty-first (#18): a confidently-wrong lookup costs more than an abstain; the kernel grounding-checker (A1 seam) should gate low-confidence retrievals. (7) R4 writes kernel vectors into activations -> needs the scoped Law-1 amendment (F1-K Gate 0) before it can run.

**Kernel Connection:** Attaches ONLY at R4, and cleanly: a memory/lookup layer IS a key-value store, so the KoT encoder's NSM-grounded concept vectors are natural KEYS and grounded per-concept content offsets are natural VALUES — the same object F1-K already constructs (per-concept definition offsets v_{c,l} as mean hidden-state differences), now indexed by retrieval instead of spliced at a fixed gate. The kernel supplies deterministic, training-free CONTENT; the distilled retrieval supplies the input-conditioning that merging destroys. This realizes the glm-kernel-grounding facet's D5 (expert-as-lookup keyed on kernel records) with the honest fix for its stated blocker (an expert is a continuous nonlinear function, not a finite table): the store approximates, retrieval conditions, and coverage is scoped to gated tokens. HARD constraint: every R4 win must beat kernel-as-text (Law 2 / F1-K d3-text arm) AND REAP-pruned+INT4 at matched disk+FLOPs; the programme's 0-for-5 runtime-interface ledger and four content-not-structure deflations mean the modal outcome is "a distilled lookup helps, the kernel content is one value-source among several." R1-R3 do NOT need the kernel — they are pure compression and stand alone.

**Cheapest Test:** Two-stage, cheapest first. STAGE 0 (CPU-minutes, no GPU, no training, no new spend): over GLM-5.2 routed-expert int4 weights + the P0/STATS routing traces already being collected, compute per-layer pairwise expert cosine similarity and the REAP collapse quantity Var[r(x)] x expert-gap for candidate clusters. Predicted (from 2406.18219 for DeepSeek-family): near-orthogonal, large gap -> R2 (merge/low-rank) is FALSIFIED analytically before any training, concentrating effort on R3. This is the single cheapest falsifier and reuses committed bytes. STAGE 1 (positive test of R3, ~1 agent-day on the already-authorized i4i.2xlarge, reusing the F1-K moe() C patch + prefill scoring): on ONE mid MoE layer (~layer 40), distil one saliency-selected cluster into a small product-key memory surrogate on a modest calibration set, splice it, and measure held-out loglikelihood NLL on GENERATION-side tokens vs (a) unmodified and (b) REAP-pruning the same experts at matched parameter budget. FALSIFIER: if the distilled lookup does not beat REAP-pruning at matched budget on generation tokens, the family is dead for GLM-5.2. Only if it passes does R4 (kernel-seeded values, behind Gate 0) become worth its Law-1 amendment.

**Prior Art:** Closest published: Memory Layers at Scale (2412.09764), PEER million-singleton-experts (2407.04153), UltraMemV2 (2508.18756) — trained-from-scratch lookup matching MoE at matched compute+params [cheap-approx/glm-kernel facets]; MC-SMoE (2310.01334) merge-by-routing-group + low-rank distill, but small fine-tuned discriminative SMoE only [pruning-merging facet]; 2605.28207 score->group->merge cluster into a dense FFN + KD (+6.3pp over dense pruning) — but distils to DENSE, not lookup; LookupFFN hashed FFN (2403.07221, small scale); Transcoders learned sparse MLP surrogate (2406.11944); Monet monosemantic-by-construction MoE (2412.04139). NOVELTY: distilling CLUSTERS of a trained, LARGE, GENERATIVE MoE (GLM-5.2) into INPUT-CONDITIONED lookup surrogates as a training-free-ish RETROFIT is unattested — the cheap-approx facet flags exactly this (its D4: "product-key memory initialized from the expert's own key-value structure") as untested/speculative with no prior art found; the interpretability facet likewise found no hand-written expert-replacement precedent. So CDL is a re-application of memory-layer + MC-SMoE ideas to the retrofit-of-frozen-large-MoE setting, novel in that specific combination, not a new primitive.

**Epistemic Tag:** Rung-dependent, honestly. R1 (hashed-KV for token-class experts) = PLAUSIBLE — grounded in LookupFFN parity, ST-MoE/OLMoE token-class specialization, and the N-gram 68-79% ceiling. R2 (cluster low-rank/merge) = LIKELY-NEGATIVE for GLM-5.2 specifically — near-orthogonal from-scratch experts + REAP's proved collapse mechanism predict failure; kept only as the Stage-0 falsifier. R3 (memory-layer retrofit) = SPECULATIVE — the parity ceiling is from-scratch-trained, no retrofit-to-frozen-large-MoE precedent exists, and prefill-over-predicts-replaceability is a live threat. R4 (kernel-seeded values) = SPECULATIVE and gated on a Law-1 amendment, carrying the programme's 0-for-5 runtime-interface and four content-not-structure priors — expect a kernel-vs-generic-content tie as the modal result. Overall: PLAUSIBLE as a compression method (beating dense-distillation's 30%-retention floor by preserving capacity is well-motivated), SPECULATIVE as a kernel vehicle. Every claim above cites a facet; the retention target is tagged STIPULATED, the compression->throughput arithmetic GROUNDED in the measured d=0.8 disk share.

### Adversarial critique — verdict: **MARGINAL**

**Feasibility Glm52:** Rung-split and mostly blocked as written. THE CENTRAL MECHANISM BUG: every rung "splices at moe() (glm.c:1270) after the top-8 weighted sum + shared expert, before residual write-back" — but that is an ADDITIVE post-sum point (the F1-K carrier location). Adding a vector after the sum cannot DELETE an expert's compute or its NVMe load; to save disk you must intercept the expert gather/stream loop and skip loading, then renormalize the router (what REAP actually does). So the post-sum splice buys additive steering with ZERO disk win; the disk/throughput thesis requires a different, un-specified code path (modify the streaming loop, not the residual write). F1-K's own REPLACE mode concedes this — its saving is "one fewer expert load per gated (token,layer)" only because it explicitly skips a load; CDL never wires that for whole clusters. RAM CEILING: the target i4i.2xlarge has ~64 GB RAM; the model is 185-370 GB on NVMe. The r->1 (5.0x) ceiling assumes a fully RAM-resident surrogate, which is physically impossible for 50% of experts (185 GB) on this box; even r=0.5 (1.67x) requires those 185 GB to NEVER be streamed for ANY token — i.e. full deletion + full-coverage surrogate, not the "covers a token fraction" framing the design uses (partial coverage means you cannot delete, so no disk win). INT4: you distill FROM already-int4-quantized experts (approximating an approximation) and must beat REAP-pruned+INT4 — already applied, already near-lossless, training-free — at matched budget, a much higher bar than beating fp16 dense. SHARED EXPERT as "ideal target" is backwards: always-on = dense input manifold (no gating to confine inputs) = the hardest lookup case, maximally triggering the design's own D5 blocker; and DeepSeekMoE's +33.5% loss when it is disabled makes it the highest-stakes, lowest-redundancy, worst place to approximate. MTP/DRAFT=0 is the only clean part — a frozen deterministic table preserves byte-reproducibility, and MTP-off means no draft-path interaction. R4 additionally needs Maintainer Gate 0 (Law-1 amendment) before it can run at all.

**Yield Realism:** The headline (>=95% retention at 50% removal with a few-GB RAM surrogate, approaching 5x) is internally inconsistent with its own cited ceiling. Memory Layers at Scale matches MoE "at matched compute+PARAMS" — parity requires comparable parameter mass, so a few-GB surrogate replacing ~185 GB of experts is ~30-90x below matched params and cannot inherit the parity number; it lands nearer Switch's "30% of the sparse gain at 99% cut." The squeeze is unavoidable: small surrogate -> partial coverage -> no deletion -> no disk win; matched-param surrogate -> tens of GB -> not RAM-resident on a 64 GB box -> still streamed. Both horns kill the 5x/1.67x story. Worse for value: REAP ALONE already gets the disk win at 92.6-97.6% (GLM-4.5-Air 0.553 vs 0.597 = 92.6%, the weakest of its set) training-free with no surrogate, so CDL's entire marginal value is "a distilled surrogate adds back the last few points" over a free, near-lossless baseline — a weak proposition against a real distillation pipeline. The only literature actually showing distill-beats-prune (2605.28207, +6.3pp) uses 4B-token KD to a DENSE FFN, not a cheap training-free lookup — so the version that plausibly hits 95% is expensive and not the lookup CDL sells. Grounded: ~50% of expert mass IS cheaply removable (REAP). Not grounded: that a cheap lookup retrofit beats REAP+INT4 at matched budget on a frozen large generative MoE — that number is STIPULATED with no supporting instance, and the params arithmetic argues against it.

**Correctness Concerns:** (1) Splice-point vs disk-win incoherence (above) — the claimed throughput win is not deliverable at the stated splice location; must move to a delete+renormalize path. (2) Loglik != autoregressive generation: at 0.09 tok/s generative eval is unaffordable, so "generation-side tokens" are scored teacher-forced via loglikelihood — which does NOT capture error accumulation under autoregressive decoding, exactly where Myth-of-Specialization (2604.09780) says pruned/collapsed experts inflict the large hit; the cheapest_test's falsifier is therefore weaker than claimed and cannot observe the failure mode it names. (3) "Retrieval dodges merging-collapse" is overstated: product-key retrieval does not preserve the trained router mixing r(x); it substitutes a NEW approximate selection r_hat(x). It avoids constant-collapse (better than merging) but introduces unbounded, un-analyzed approximation error against r(x); "preserves input-conditioning" is a hand-wave, not the REAP theorem's negation. (4) GLM/DeepSeek-family from-scratch experts are near-orthogonal (pairwise cos ~0, 2406.18219) -> maximal expert-gap -> R2 is analytically dead (the design agrees) AND makes R3's distillation target high-variance/hard. (5) Super-expert inclusion -> catastrophic degeneration (2507.23279); needs a data-agnostic exclusion pass as a hard precondition. (6) Calibration-domain conditioning (REAP): c4-calibrated pruning produced incoherent code; the surrogate inherits the calibration corpus's blind spots. (7) R1's virtue is orthogonal to the goal: clean token-class/operational experts are a small, frequency-biased, often-polysemantic minority (Expert Strikes Back auto-labelling fails on them), so a correct hashed-KV for them yields a tiny disk fraction — safe but near-worthless for the 370 GB thesis. (8) R4 writes kernel vectors into activations (Law-1 amendment) and its own modal outcome is a tie with generic content.

**Prior Art Overlap:** High component overlap; the novel cell is precisely the unproven one. Identification = REAP conditional-impact saliency (2510.13999), unchanged. Cluster-distill = 2605.28207 (score->group->merge cluster + KD), which distills to DENSE and is better-supported than CDL's lookup variant — CDL is arguably dominated by "just run 2605.28207 on GLM-5.2 clusters" for the compression claim. Surrogate concept = Transcoders (2406.11944), which already replace a whole MLP with a learned sparse surrogate — R3 is transcoders-at-scale-as-memory-layer, so the "training-free" advantage is largely illusory (R3's offline distillation IS a training pass; only inference is table-lookup, same as transcoders). Lookup structure = Memory Layers (2412.09764)/PEER (2407.04153)/UltraMemV2 — all trained-from-scratch, matched-params, NOT retrofits. R2 (merge/low-rank) = MC-SMoE (2310.01334), refuted at large-generative scale by REAP Thm 1. Net: CDL is a recombination whose genuinely new cell — "training-free-ish input-conditioned lookup RETROFIT of a frozen large generative MoE" — is novel because no one has shown the components compose to beat REAP+INT4, not because it is unattempted. The interpretability facet's absence-of-evidence on hand-written expert replacement holds; the design correctly forbids hand-authoring, but that pushes it back into the trained-surrogate camp where transcoders/2605.28207 already sit.

**Strengthened Version:** Strip CDL to a defensible compression probe and demote the kernel to a separate gated ablation. (1) DROP R2 except as the free Stage-0 analytic falsifier it already is. (2) STOP selling it as a kernel vehicle — R1-R3 are pure MoE compression with no kernel; the kernel (R4) is a later, Gate-0-gated value-source ABLATION, pre-registered against F1-K's d3-text control AND a norm-matched generic value store, with a kernel-vs-generic TIE as the honest modal prediction. (3) FIX the mechanism: any disk/throughput claim must be implemented as REAP-style expert DELETION + router renormalization + a surrogate that FULLY covers the deleted routing — not a post-sum additive splice. Pick ONE regime and state it: either small-surrogate/partial-coverage (compute/steering win only, abandon the 185 GB/5x claims) or full-deletion/matched-param surrogate (accept tens of GB, not RAM-resident on i4i.2xlarge, so the ceiling is a smaller-but-still-streamed store, not RAM lookup). (4) Set the RIGHT incumbent: the baseline to beat is REAP-50% + INT4 (near-lossless, free), NOT fp16 unmodified — so the only honest question is "does a cheap surrogate convert REAP's near-lossless into actually-lossless at matched total params?", a small claim. (5) Use the BEST-SUPPORTED surrogate first: dense KD per 2605.28207 (which has the only positive distill-over-prune number), and only escalate to product-key/lookup if dense wins and disk pressure demands it. (6) TARGET low-salience ROUTED expert clusters where REAP deletion is already near-lossless — NOT the shared expert (dense manifold, +33.5% stakes, worst lookup case) and NOT late layers (output-token routing). (7) Harden the eval: teacher-forced continuation NLL over-predicts replaceability, so the Stage-1 falsifier MUST include a small, budgeted autoregressive-generation sample despite the 0.09 tok/s cost, or it cannot observe the accumulation failure it worries about. Stage-0 (analytic Var[r(x)]xgap + expert cosine over already-committed traces) remains an excellent, free R2 killer and a legitimate reason to run this at all. Bottom line: as a hedged compression research probe with these fixes it is worth a Stage-0 + one-layer Stage-1 against REAP+INT4; as a kernel-shrinking north-star it is a distant, content-not-structure-deflated bet that should not gate programme resources.

---

## Deterministic Routing Short-Circuit (DRS)
**One-liner:** Route on cheap token features instead of loading experts — prefetch what's predictable, and for structural token-classes whose routing AND output are near-constant, memoize the MoE output and never touch disk.

**Mechanism:** GLM-5.2 in colibri: 78 layers, first_k_dense_replace=3 (layers 0-2 dense FFN, no routing — DRS applies only to the ~75 MoE layers 3-77), 256 routed experts/layer, greedy top-8, an always-on shared expert, sigmoid+bias loss-free-balance router, MTP head at layer 78 (int8; DRAFT=0 = MTP off → greedy byte-reproducible, which makes both the DRS-0/1 byte-identity invariant and the DRS-2 retention eval clean). The router gate itself is cheap+resident (part of the ~17B dense in RAM); the cost is streaming 19 MB expert weights off NVMe (~11.4 GB/token, 80% of wall-time [F1-K §1.3]). So the short-circuit targets the DISK read, not the gate matmul. Build an offline deterministic predictor P(top-8 set | token-features) from the F1-K P0 routing-trace dataset (per-token per-layer expert IDs). Rungs: (DRS-0) predict the token's expert set from features available BEFORE the attention block (deeper horizon than colibri PILOT's 1-layer post-attention predictor, which is 71.6% predictable one layer ahead [GLM52-NS §1.1]) and prefetch; router stays authoritative → byte-identical, pure overlap. (DRS-1) partition each token into 'resident-routing' (predicted top-8 ⊆ the static hot set, which already serves 0.76 of loads [MEASURED, F1-K §1.3]) vs 'streaming'; for resident tokens, suppress speculative/prefetch disk requests entirely and reallocate I/O-thread bandwidth to streaming tokens — still byte-identical. (DRS-2, the only rung that removes bytes) for a whitelist of structural token-classes (whitespace/indentation, punctuation, articles/conjunctions — ST-MoE token-class experts [what-experts-do item2]; OLMoE late-layer vocabulary experts [item3]; operational experts like LaTeX bracket-closing [what-experts-do item6]) whose routing is near-deterministic (Mixtral: indentation tokens and 'self' in Python pin to one expert; consecutive-token same-expert 14%→28% layers 0→15 [item1]) AND whose routed-expert output is low-variance, replace the MoE sublayer output with a cached vector keyed on (token-class, layer) and skip both router and expert-load. GLM-5.2-specific mechanistic argument for why the routed contribution is memoizable on structural tokens: DeepSeekMoE's always-on shared expert carries the 'fundamental/common' knowledge (disabling it costs +0.606 Pile loss [what-experts-do item4]) — it fires unconditionally and stays resident — so for a whitespace/punctuation token the shared expert already supplies the generic component and the routed top-8 adds only a small, near-constant residual, which is exactly what a (token-class, layer) memo can absorb. Implementation rides the F1-K envelope: predictor feeds a sidecar (like KAE=spans) into PIN/PILOT; DRS-2 is a ~40-80 line skip-branch in moe() at glm.c:1270 with a memo table, analogous to the F1-K splice patch, under the same MAINTAINER GATE 0 fork discipline.

**Target Experts:** Not concept/domain experts — the LOW-ENTROPY structural ones the routing-analysis literature identifies as predictable: (a) token-class experts — punctuation, article/conjunction, number, proper-noun, sentinel [ST-MoE, what-experts-do item2]; (b) vocabulary/formatting experts firing on scripts, units, indentation, bracket-closing [OLMoE item3; The Expert Strikes Back operational family item6]; (c) whatever the F1-K static hot set already concentrates (0.76 of loads MEASURED). Identified by pure offline analysis of the P0 routing traces: (1) per-(token-feature, layer) routing ENTROPY — how deterministic is the top-8 given only cheap surface features (token id, prev token, indentation state, position class) — this selects the DRS-0/1 predictable slots; (2) per-(token-class, layer) MoE-OUTPUT variance × REAP-style conditional-impact saliency (gate-value × activation-norm, computed conditional on activation [pruning-merging, REAP 2510.13999]) — this selects the DRS-2 memoization whitelist (memoize only where both output variance and downstream impact are below a floor). MANDATORY EXCLUSION LIST: numeric/math tokens (DM-Mathematics was the ONE domain where Mixtral routing deviated from uniform [what-experts-do item1] — memoizing digits corrupts arithmetic); input-token-keyed memoization in LATE layers (OLMoE: late layers route on the predicted OUTPUT token, not the input token [item3] — input-keyed memo mispredicts structurally, so DRS-2 is restricted to early/mid structural layers); and Super-Experts (3 of 6144 down_proj outlier carriers whose removal collapses the model [pruning-merging 2507.23279]) which the whitelist must never touch.

**Expected Yield:** GROUNDED: the predictability PRIORS are established — static-pin 0.76 hit [MEASURED, F1-K §1.3], PILOT 71.6% one-layer [GLM52-NS §1.1], OLMoE router saturation 60% at 20B tokens / 80% by 40% [what-experts-do item3]. GROUNDED arithmetic: on the probe box eliminating fraction r of expert-load bytes multiplies throughput by 1/(0.2+0.8(1−r)) (Amdahl, disk share 0.8), ceiling 5× at r=1 [F1-K §1.3]. STIPULATED (no measured retention or marginal-hit number): DRS-0/1 are byte-identical so accuracy retention = 1.0 by construction, but their THROUGHPUT yield ≈ 0 on the probe's saturated disk — colibri reports PILOT MEASURED-neutral on saturated disks [GLM52-NS §1.2.2], and prefetch only overlaps I/O, it does not reduce bytes; DRS-0/1 pay off ONLY on balanced hardware (fast disk + strong CPU) where overlap has headroom. DRS-2 is the only rung that reduces bytes: STIPULATED throughput ~1.05–1.15× on prose, ~1.1–1.5× on code/structured workloads (where structural+indentation tokens can be 30–50% of tokens and their routing is most deterministic [Mixtral, item1]) — grounded ONLY in a structural-token-fraction estimate × the routing-determinism literature, with retention UNMEASURED. The binding deflator that caps all of this: static-pinning already captures 0.76, so the marginal 'predictable AND not-already-resident' fraction may be small; the whole family's payoff is the size of that residual, which is exactly what the cheapest test measures. ALL throughput/retention figures STIPULATED; only the predictability priors and the Amdahl arithmetic are grounded.

**Correctness Risk:** DRS-0 and DRS-1 carry ZERO correctness risk — the router remains authoritative and greedy DRAFT=0 output is byte-identical (a mechanical SHA-256 invariant over token ids voids any run that diverges, mirroring F1-A §4.4). DRS-2 carries real risk: memoizing a token-class output where context actually matters corrupts generation. The concrete failure modes, each with a literature-anchored hazard: (i) numeric/math tokens — Mixtral's DM-Mathematics was the single domain where routing deviated [item1], so a memoized digit or operator breaks arithmetic; (ii) disambiguating punctuation (a comma/period that changes parse) whose 'structural' class label hides context sensitivity; (iii) late-layer memoization keyed on input token, which mispredicts because late layers route on the predicted output token [OLMoE item3]; (iv) accidental inclusion of a Super-Expert carrier [2507.23279] → collapse. Guards: the REAP-style conditional-impact whitelist (never memoize where output-variance × saliency exceeds a pre-registered floor), the hard exclusion list above, restriction of DRS-2 to early/mid layers, and a DRAFT=0 loglikelihood retention gate (prefill-scored, the F1-K §2.7 mitigation) that must show non-inferiority vs the unmodified model before any memo whitelist ships. Off-whitelist tokens are byte-identical, so the blast radius is bounded to the whitelisted structural classes.

**Kernel Connection:** HONEST: this family is largely KERNEL-FREE, and that is its most important property. Token-syntax routing determinism is a surface-form phenomenon (Hash-Layers: fixed random token-hash routing is competitive with learned routing [what-experts-do item7 / cheap-approximation d]; Mixtral token-identity pins [item1]) — it needs no NSM concepts, so the KoT concept-kernel does NOT attach at the prediction layer. Two genuine connections remain. (1) DEFLATOR ROLE: DRS is the strongest kernel-free routing short-circuit, i.e. it IS the a1/a2-class null (AUTOPIN + embedding-cluster) that the concept-conditioned levers B1a/F1-A must beat at matched RAM budget [GLM52-NS §2.1 deflator ladder; glm-kernel-grounding]. Building DRS well sharpens the bar the kernel routing claim has to clear, and on the programme's four content-not-structure deflations the modal outcome is that concept-conditioning ties DRS — in which case DRS is the reportable systems result and the kernel earns nothing on this seam. (2) CORRECTNESS-GATE ROLE (optional): the RULES-1 deterministic engine can act as a grounding-checker that VETOES memoization on any token whose local context the engine flags as semantically load-bearing (an entailed fact, a typed slot) — Logic-LM-style engine-owns-correctness [glm-kernel-grounding, Logic-LM +39.2%]. The kernel/engine drives nothing in DRS; it competes with it as the null and optionally gates DRS-2 for safety.

**Cheapest Test:** Pure offline re-analysis of the EXISTING F1-K P0 routing-trace dataset (per-token, per-layer expert IDs, already committed) — $0 new instance spend, CPU-minutes, re-analysable forever (house delegate-and-reanalyse discipline). FALSIFIER-1 (DRS-0/1): regress the top-8 set (and the resident-vs-streaming partition) on cheap token features (token id, prev token, indentation, position class); if per-(token-feature, layer) routing is NOT predictable beyond what the static 0.76 hot set already captures — i.e. the marginal 'predictable AND not-already-resident' load fraction is below a pre-registered floor (~5%) — then DRS-0/1 are dead on this model, no capex spent. FALSIFIER-2 (DRS-2, needs the F1-K dump mode already specified in F1-K §2.8, ~$20 on the warm probe box): dump MoE-OUTPUT vectors for the candidate structural token-classes across many contexts and compute per-(token-class, layer) output coefficient-of-variation; if it exceeds a pre-set floor (outputs are context-dependent), memoization is dead before any generation run. Both falsifiers reuse the F1-K probe box + patch and precede any live throughput A/B; the live confirmation (memoized vs baseline, byte-diff + loglik retention) only fires if both pass. Kill-cheap-first, exactly the G-B1 admission-gate pattern.

**Prior Art:** Closest deployed analogue: colibri PILOT (71.6% one-layer, hidden-state prefetch predictor [GLM52-NS §1.1]) and Pre-gated MoE (arXiv:2308.12066, cited GLM52-NS §2.1) — DRS-0/1 are a deterministic-token-feature, deeper-horizon RE-APPLICATION of pre-gating/prefetch. Hash Layers (Roller et al. NeurIPS'21, arXiv:2106.04426 [cheap-approximation d]) is the licence that routing CAN be a fixed function of the token, here repurposed from training-time routing to inference-time PREDICTION of an already-trained router. Activation-locality caching — MoE-Infinity (2401.14361), HOBBIT (2411.01433, cold-expert INT4/INT2), Mixtral-offloading (2312.17238) [pruning-merging §1] — is adjacent to DRS-1's resident partition. REAP's conditional-impact saliency (gate×activation-norm, arXiv:2510.13999 [pruning-merging]) is DRS-2's whitelist gate, applied per-token-class rather than per-expert-globally. The nearest licence for DRS-2's memoization is N-gram rulesets matching 79%/68% of transformer top-1 next-token predictions (Nguyen 2407.12034 [interpretability]) and LookupFFN (2403.07221 [cheap-approximation f]). NOVEL element (no published precedent found across the interpretability + cheap-approximation facets, both flagged a null on hand-written/deterministic expert replacement): training-free MEMOIZED-MoE-output-by-token-class used as an inference-time DISK short-circuit on a streaming MoE. Net: DRS-0/1 = established re-application (honest-null-prone); DRS-2 = novel combination.

**Epistemic Tag:** MIXED, tagged per rung. That routing is predictable from cheap token features = ESTABLISHED (Mixtral token-identity + consecutive-locality 14→28% [item1]; OLMoE saturation 60–80% [item3]; Hash-Layers competitiveness [item7]; PILOT 71.6%; static-pin 0.76 MEASURED). That this predictability yields DISK-BYTE savings BEYOND the incumbent static-pin+LRU+AUTOPIN at matched RAM = PLAUSIBLE-leaning-SPECULATIVE — the 0.76 incumbent is a strong deflator and the marginal residual is unmeasured (the cheapest test exists precisely to size it). DRS-2 memoization RETAINING quality = SPECULATIVE — no retention number, real hazards (DM-Math routing deviation, late-layer output-keyed routing, super-experts), and GLM-5.2's 256-expert top-8 regime raises routing entropy vs Mixtral's top-2-of-8, making full-set prediction harder (offset by the shared-expert-absorbs-common-knowledge argument, itself only PLAUSIBLE). Overall: ESTABLISHED-but-likely-neutral for the byte-identical prefetch rungs on a saturated disk; SPECULATIVE for the byte-removing memoization rung that actually pays. Load-bearing STIPULATED premise: that a predictable-and-not-already-resident residual large enough to beat static-pin exists — unregistered, falsified cheaply by FALSIFIER-1.

### Adversarial critique — verdict: **MARGINAL**

**Feasibility Glm52:** Split by rung. DRS-0/1 are mechanically feasible on colibri int4 — they ride the existing PIN/PILOT sidecar, keep the router authoritative, and are byte-identical under DRAFT=0 (SHA-256 guard). But their throughput yield is ~0 on the ONLY hardware the programme has measured: NS §1.2.2 states plainly that prefetch does NOT reduce bytes read (it only overlaps I/O) and that colibri's PILOT is measured NEUTRAL on saturated disks. The i4i.2xlarge probe (disk = 80% of wall-time, 3.33 GB/s, 0.09 tok/s) IS a saturated disk. So DRS-0/1 pay off only on "balanced hardware (fast disk + strong CPU)" that the programme does not run — their entire yield is extrapolated to unavailable hardware. A specific misattribution: the design lists "PILOT 71.6% one-layer" as a GROUNDED predictability prior for DRS, but that 71.6% is the accuracy of predicting layer L+1 from the POST-ATTENTION HIDDEN STATE, not from surface features (token id / prev token / indentation). DRS's surface-feature predictor is a different, unmeasured object; Hash-Layers licenses that a router CAN be a token-hash at TRAINING time, not that a trained sigmoid top-8-of-256 router is recoverable from surface features at inference. GLM-5.2's top-8-of-256 regime (vs Mixtral top-2-of-8) inflates full-set entropy combinatorially, which the design concedes. DRS-2 is implementable as a ~40-80 line moe() skip-branch, but three GLM-5.2-specific frictions bite: (i) DRAFT>0 (the production speed regime) already amortizes expert loads via the union of drafted tokens' expert sets per forward (NS §1.2 pt 3), shrinking DRS-2's marginal byte-saving — yet the design measures throughput at DRAFT=0, the slower non-production path; (ii) the "shared expert absorbs the generic component" mechanistic argument rests on DeepSeekMoE's +0.606 Pile-loss number, unverified on GLM-5.2's shared expert; (iii) if the whole MoE output (incl. shared) is memoized, the always-on common-knowledge contribution is deleted, not just the routed residual.

**Yield Realism:** Optimistic even in its own terms, and dominated by an existing baseline. The 5x Amdahl figure is the r=1 (all expert bytes eliminated) unreachable ceiling. The binding deflator the design itself names is decisive: static pinning already serves 0.76 of loads (MEASURED), so the exploitable residual is at most ~0.24, and the "surface-predictable AND not-already-resident AND safely-memoizable" slice is a fraction of that fraction. A realistic DRS-2 byte fraction — (structural-token share ~0.3-0.5) × (early/mid layers only ~0.5) × (not-already-resident ~0.24) ≈ 0.05 — gives 1/(0.2+0.8·0.95) ≈ 1.04x, BELOW the design's own STIPULATED 1.05-1.15x prose figure. Against the literature ceiling this is worse: REAP (2510.13999, full-text verified) achieves 50% training-free expert removal at ~2-3% quality cost (GLM-4.5-Air weakest at ~7% relative), i.e. r=0.5 → 1.67x throughput AND a disk-GB win, already published and simpler. REAP strictly dominates DRS-2 on yield (1.67x vs ~1.04x), risk, and novelty. The published training-free MoE-compression ceiling is ~40-50% removal (REAP/STUN); per-token structural memoization delivers a small fraction of that while carrying quality risk REAP does not. DRS-0/1 throughput is honestly ~0 on the measured saturated disk. Net: the only grounded quantities are the predictability PRIORS and the Amdahl arithmetic; every throughput/retention figure is STIPULATED, and the realistic ones are sub-measurement-dignity.

**Correctness Concerns:** DRS-0/1: zero — router stays authoritative, a wrong prediction only costs a cache miss, byte-identity is SHA-256-enforced. DRS-2 carries real, partly fundamental risk beyond the four hazards the design already lists (DM-Math tokens, late-layer output-token routing, disambiguating punctuation, super-experts). (1) Core conflation: routing determinism ≠ OUTPUT determinism. The MoE output is a function of the continuous, fully-contextualized hidden state, not the discrete token-class; a whitespace token after `def foo(` and one mid-prose share a class but not a hidden state, so a (class, layer) memo collapses genuinely distinct inputs to one vector. FALSIFIER-2 (output CoV) is the right test, but the expected-yield section pre-assumes it passes via a hand-wave. (2) The N-gram 79%/68% licence is for the FINAL next-token distribution; a MID-STACK sublayer memo propagates through 30-55 subsequent layers and greedy argmax is discontinuous, so even a low-CoV residual can flip the output token — retention is a real quality tradeoff, not free-by-construction. (3) "Structural = low-entropy" is false in code: Python indentation IS control flow and brackets ARE nesting depth; memoizing exactly these tokens can corrupt the model's structure tracking — the highest-value workload for the claimed 1.1-1.5x is where the correctness risk is worst. (4) The merging-collapse theorem (REAP Thm 1) applies in the limit: memoize-to-constant per class is per-class merge-to-constant, with irreducible error ∝ Var[r(x)] × expert-gap, proven to collapse on generative tasks (HC-SMoE 0.008 WildBench @ 50%). The CoV whitelist only searches the near-zero-variance corner — so DRS-2 is trapped between "variance low enough to be safe → yield tiny" and "yield material → variance corrupts." (5) The retention gate is prefill/loglik-scored, but NS §1 warns prefill routing over-predicts replaceability and the real hit shows at GENERATION — the gate must validate on generation, not prefill. (6) Keep the shared expert LIVE; memoizing the full MoE output deletes common knowledge.

**Prior Art Overlap:** DRS-0/1 is not novel: it is Pre-gated MoE (2308.12066) + colibri PILOT + Hash-Layers (2106.04426) re-application, and the design admits this — "established re-application, honest-null-prone." DRS-1's resident partition overlaps MoE-Infinity / HOBBIT / Mixtral-offloading activation-locality caching. DRS-2's claimed novelty ("training-free memoized-MoE-output-by-token-class as a disk short-circuit") is real as a specific combination, but it sits adjacent to contextual-sparsity/activation caching (Deja Vu), LookupFFN, and N-gram rulesets, and it IS the constant limit of expert merging — precisely the regime REAP Thm 1 and HC-SMoE prove collapses on large generative MoEs. So its "novelty" overlaps heavily with "refuted-in-the-limit": no one memoizes mid-stack outputs to per-class constants because reconstruction ≠ function (the same reason plain SVD-FFN truncation and merging fail). The routing-is-token-identity result (Mixtral 2401.04088) and Hash-Layers license the predictor's EXISTENCE, not its ACCURACY against a trained top-8-of-256 router from surface features — that accuracy is the unmeasured crux.

**Strengthened Version:** Collapse the family to its $0 core plus one deflator arm, and drop DRS-2 as a throughput lever. (1) Run FALSIFIER-1 as pure offline re-analysis of the already-committed P0 routing traces ($0, CPU-minutes): measure (a) surface-feature predictability of the trained top-8-of-256 router and (b) the marginal predictable-AND-not-already-resident load fraction ABOVE the 0.76 static hot set. This single number sizes the headroom for EVERY routing lever — kernel and non-kernel — at zero spend, and honestly gates the whole family; if the residual is below a pre-registered floor (~5%), the byte-saving story is dead on this model with no capex. (2) If it clears the floor, DRS's genuine marginal contribution is to add ONE "surface-feature pin/skip" arm to the ALREADY-BUDGETED F1-A A/B (§4), sitting as a stronger kernel-free deflator between AUTOPIN (a1) and the oracle (a5). This tightens the bar the kernel a3-concept-pin must beat, at ~$0 marginal on the warm box, byte-identical — which is exactly the design's own most-honest framing of itself (the a1/a2-class null the kernel must clear). (3) Never report DRS-0/1 as a throughput win on the saturated i4i box; label them balanced-hardware-only and out of scope until such hardware exists. (4) If DRS-2 is pursued at all, restrict it to: keep the shared expert LIVE and memoize ONLY the routed residual (so the shared-expert-carries-common-knowledge argument actually holds); a tiny CoV-verified EARLY/MID-layer whitelist excluding numeric/code-structural tokens and super-expert carriers; a GENERATION-time (not prefill-only) retention non-inferiority gate; and a pre-registered requirement to BEAT REAP-50%-pruned + int4 at matched disk — a bar it is expected to LOSE, so book it as a documented negative that sharpens the REAP baseline, not as a lever. Defensible net claim: DRS is a kernel-free systems NULL whose value is measurement — a $0 headroom number and a tightened deflator for the kernel routing seam — not throughput.

---
